title: Customer Insights Analyst
description: Customer Insights Analyst 12\-Month Contract Hybrid \| 3 Days Onsite \| Dublin Connexus Recruit are partnering with a growing Irish consumer brand group to hire a Customer Insights Analyst for a newly created role focused on customer, market and commercial insight. This is a highly visible role sitting within the marketing function, helping shape business strategy through customer understanding, behavioural insight and market intelligence. The business operates across multiple well\-known consumer brands and is investing heavily in customer experience, engagement and long\-term growth. They are looking for someone who can turn complex data into clear commercial recommendations and influence decision\-making across the organisation. **The Role** This role will focus on building a stronger understanding of customer behaviour, market trends and commercial performance to support strategic growth initiatives. You will lead insight and research activity across customer, competitor and market data, helping the business make more informed decisions around customer engagement, retention, brand positioning and future opportunities. You will also play a key role in shaping a new end\-to\-end customer engagement programme, helping define how the business better understands, supports and retains its customer base over time. **Key Responsibilities** * Lead customer, market and competitor insight initiatives * Analyse qualitative and quantitative data to uncover actionable business opportunities * Develop dashboards, KPI frameworks and insight reporting for senior stakeholders * Translate complex datasets into clear commercial narratives and recommendations * Track customer behaviour, retention, engagement and satisfaction metrics * Support customer segmentation and lifecycle engagement initiatives * Identify trends, risks and growth opportunities across the market * Partner cross\-functionally with marketing, commercial and leadership teams **What They’re Looking For** * 3\+ years’ experience in insights, analytics, business intelligence or consumer research * Strong experience working with customer or commercial data * Ability to turn data into meaningful business recommendations * Strong stakeholder communication and presentation skills * Experience with tools such as Power BI, SQL, Excel or similar analytics platforms * Background in retail, ecommerce, FMCG or consumer\-led environments is essential * Experience across customer insights, segmentation, retention or lifecycle analysis would be advantageous **Why This Role?** * Opportunity to shape a newly evolving insights function * High visibility role with influence across commercial and marketing strategy * Strong mix of strategic thinking, customer insight and commercial impact * Collaborative and forward\-thinking environment

title: Customer Operations Specialist
description: **Your new company** We are recruiting on behalf of one of Ireland's largest financial institutions. The organisation provides a wide range of banking, financial, and related services. This company is customer\-focussed and dynamic, driven by empowered people with an agile mind\-set and approach, with teams executing brilliantly every day, consistently outperforming the market and generating value. There is a current vacancy for a Customer Operations Specialist to be based in a busy office in Central Park Dublin 18\. **Your new role** The role is to be part of a large and dynamic fast paced team, with responsibility to work within guidelines to achieve agreed daily targets, ensuring information is complete, accurate and readily available.The successful candidate should be a highly organised, motivated, energised \& proactive individual capable of working on own initiative within a team structure and possess excellent communication \& interpersonal skills. **What you'll need to succeed** * Maintain a clear focus on agreed goals to achieve expected results. * Self\-starter with ability to work on own initiative with a strong attention to detail. * Excellent interpersonal and communication skills (written and verbal) and confidence to interact with all levels of management on a regular basis. * Understand the need to provide the highest quality customer service, deliver to customer expectations and act with total integrity. * Strong time management skills with the ability to prioritise tasks. **What you'll get in return** * Excellent on the job and learning opportunities. * You will be joining a diverse and inclusive company whose customers and employees are at the forefront of everything they do. * You will receive a very competitive hourly rate with an annual holiday accrual. **What you need to do now** If you're interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up\-to\-date copy of your CV, or call us now. If this job isn't quite right for you, but you are looking for a new position, please contact us for a confidential discussion about your career. Hays Ireland is a trading division of Hays Specialist Recruitment (Ireland) Limited and acts as an employment agency for permanent recruitment and employment business for the supply of temporary workers. By applying for this job you accept the T\&C's, Privacy Policy and Disclaimers which can be found at hays.co.uk

title: Data Centre Operations Integration Technician
description: **Job Title:** Data Centre Operations Integration Technician **Location:** Clondalkin **Work Times** : Monday to Friday (8am – 5pm) **PURPOSE OF THE JOB** To manage risk and build technical knowledge during the handover of construction builds \& be the operational integration between the site operations \& the construction/CX team. The Operations Integration Tech will report to the Operational Integration supervisor, and the team will ensure that all construction activities are handed over in such a way that mitigates and minimises risk to the operational Site Operations Team Summarised below are the main tasks that we anticipate would fall within the remit of this position. **Key Responsibilities** * Work as part of the Operations Integration Team and ensure successful integration of new buildings on Campus to the Site Operations team. * Develop an in\-depth knowledge of the technical systems * Generate operational documentation to allow the Site Operations Team to operate and maintain the equipment safely and effectively. **Interface Works with existing Operations** * Audit and challenge subcontractor works in the operational environment * Adherence to all quality, health \& safety, and environmental policies within the Data Centre domain * Ensure the development of site related procedures including EOPs, SOPs, MOPs, DRILLS as part of the facility handover to operations. All documentation should be consistent with current client standards. * Develop switching schedules in conjunction with the Technical Supervisors where shutdowns are required * Business / Ops Risk Assessments and Control Measures * Health and Safety Risk Assessments and Control Measures * Process and Approve CMOPs / Method Statements submitted * Attendance on Shutdowns and isolations during construction/commissioning as required * Generation of technical reports detailing the activities and issues relevant to the Transition Team for the Client \& operations as required. * Report health and safety breaches through the ‘HAZARD’ process * Ensure the professional image of CBRE is presented to clients and visitors at all times. * Develop a good working relationship with all members of CBRE staff and the Client representatives. * Ensure the provision of a Safe and Healthy working environment. To include the wearing of the CBRE uniform at all times and appropriate PPE as required. **For all new Builds (DUB’s)** * Act as “operational advocate” for start\-up and commissioning activities. * Attendance at Cx/Start\-up Meetings * Attendance at Integration Meetings * Attendance on Commissioning / walk downs * Attendance of SLT/IST, \& provide feedback on issues identified * O\&M Reviews and Sign off * Assist in the coordination of Vendor Training Programme for Technical \& Operations Shift team * Snagging / Defect Reporting * Warranty Management (in conjunction with the client) * Critical Spares – Assess requirements for new builds * Development of detailed emergency, maintenance, and drill procedures. **Qualifications, Experience And Character** * A fully verifiable trade qualification in a relevant engineering discipline and verifiable additional training that is relevant to the proposed environment. * Experience at Technician level in a critical environment (preference is given to data center, hospital, pharmaceutical production or power plant experience). * Capability to manage escalations and emergency issues which contain various degrees of ambiguity and either resolve or properly escalate in a timely manner, where appropriate * An excellent understanding and experience of the electrical and mechanical systems used in a data center environment, including: Feeders, Transformers, Generators, Fuel Systems, Switchgear, UPS systems, Battery banks, ATS/STS units, PDU/PMM units, Chillers, Air handling units, CRAC units, Fire Alarm systems, and Fire Suppression systems. * Experience of complex automatic control equipment, including relay logic, programmable logic controllers (PLC’s), building management systems, and their integration with the data center infrastructure * Experience of medium voltage distribution systems and associated switch gear and protection equipment * Previous experience of working within a Project Environment and dealing with design and construction engineering professionals * Strong oral and written communication skills, with the ability to communicate intelligently and effectively with the client and with contractors who perform maintenance or upgrade work on the data center systems * Computer literate with the ability to compile and submit monthly and annual reports, and to monitor and trend operational characteristics (load, capacity, environmental conditions etc.). Good working ability on Microsoft Office Products, in particular Excel, Word, Outlook and PowerPoint. * The ability to learn quickly and to gain maximum benefit from extensive training and development programs aimed at ensuring that any identified knowledge or experience deficiencies are addressed in the short term * Organised, able to effectively prioritise and deliver within a dynamic and high pressure, business critical environments * Self\-motivated, with ability to work on own initiative * Must have a high level of energy, be self\- starters, confident and stable in manner * Must understand what a Data Centre environment is, and the working restrictions applicable to such environments. * Must be able to demonstrate a career showing continuous personal development with at least two years’ experience in production or critical environments Please note All details are provided for guidance only, and do not necessarily limit the responsibilities and accountabilities of the job. Full details of employment terms and conditions are provided within Offers of Employment, The Employee Handbook and appropriate policies within the Company. Why CBRE When you join CBRE, you become part of the global leader in commercial real estate services and investment that helps businesses and people thrive. We are dynamic problem solvers and forward\-thinking professionals who create significant impact. Our collaborative culture is built on our shared values — respect, integrity, service and excellence — and we value the diverse perspectives, backgrounds and skillsets of our people. At CBRE, you have the opportunity to chart your own course and realize your potential. We welcome all applicants. Applicant AI Use Disclosure We value human interaction to understand each candidate's unique experience, skills and aspirations. We do not use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to make hiring decisions, and we ask that candidates disclose any use of AI in the application and interview process.

title: Data Scientist I
description: Job Description: **Description** Are you passionate about data and eager to make a meaningful impact within a dynamic organisation? AXA is seeking a Junior Data Scientist to join our team and provide data analytics support and innovative solutions that drive our business forward. In this role, you will have the opportunity to work on a variety of exciting projects, as well as the Analysis and Integration of External data sources. If you are ready to kickstart your career in data science and contribute to our mission of delivering exceptional value to our customers, we want to hear from you! At AXA we work smart, empowering our people to balance their time between home and the office in a way that works best for them, their team and our customers. You'll work majority of your week away from home. Away from home means either attendance at one of our head office locations, visiting clients or attending industry events. **What you’ll be doing:*** Support the delivery of Data Analytics projects under supervision. * Understand and assess the needs and requirements of internal customers. * Analyse large datasets and provide ad\-hoc analyses with data interpretation. * Apply modelling techniques and effectively present findings to audiences with varying technical expertise. * Maintain technical documentation and perform data control checks to ensure accuracy. * Demonstrate strong analytical and problem\-solving skills with excellent attention to detail. * Exhibit self\-motivation, flexibility, and a commitment to deliver on objectives while adhering to AXA’s compliance standards. **About You/What we’re looking for:*** A bachelor's degree with a strong focus on mathematics and statistics is required. * Previous experience in data analysis and modelling role, with previous experience in an insurance or financial services company desirable. * Statistical background and proficiency in programming using large datasets. * Experience in programming in SAS or R, that would be beneficial. * Being skilled in Excel, Word, and PowerPoint is desirable with excellent written and numerical skills. As a precondition of employment for this role, you must be eligible and authorized to work in Ireland and/or the United Kingdom. This recruitment campaign will be used to support permanent, temporary and/or extension for any upcoming similar positions.

title: Data, Commercial and Technology Lawyer
description: We are working with a top\-tier cross\-border law firm based in Dublin for a Lawyer to join their Data, Commercial and Technology team. **The Role:** The successful candidate will join a leading Data, Commercial and Technology team advising a broad range of international and institutional clients, including hedge fund managers, private equity firms and multinational corporations. The role involves providing advice on data protection, GDPR compliance and investigations, AI, intellectual property, and the data and technology aspects of complex commercial transactions, including joint ventures and mergers and acquisitions. The position also includes drafting and negotiating a wide range of commercial contracts and supporting high\-value, cross\-border transactions in collaboration with corporate and employment teams. **Essential Criteria:** * 3\-6 years’ PQE; * Data, Commercial and Technology background; * Excellent academics; * Strong drafting abilities; * Interpersonal skills; * Strong communication skills; * Team player; * Technically excellent. **Benefits:** * Excellent salary package (commensurate with experience); * Hybrid working; * Attractive annual leave; * Health coverage; * International work; * Opportunity to progress within the firm. Please contact Andrew Currie on 0892015064 or email andrew.currie@achuman.com if you would like to have a confidential discussion about this opportunity.

title: DevOps Engineer
description: **DevOps Engineers \| Kubernetes, AWS, Azure \& GCP \| Multi\-Cloud Platform Build** * Dublin Docklands, 4 days onsite, 1 day from home * €70,000 \- €110,000 base \+ bonus Our client is a global software company that has been delivering mission\-critical technology platforms for more than 45 years across healthcare, finance, logistics and government sectors. Their systems underpin large\-scale environments where uptime, scalability, resilience and security are essential. As part of a major cloud transformation programme, they’re building out a brand\-new DevOps and cloud platform engineering function in Dublin and are hiring multiple engineers across Senior, Lead and Principal\-level roles. This team will focus on designing, automating and operating large\-scale Kubernetes and cloud\-native infrastructure across AWS, Azure and GCP, supporting globally distributed SaaS and managed service platforms. Depending on your level, you’ll either be deeply hands\-on across automation, Kubernetes and infrastructure engineering, or operating as a senior technical leader shaping architecture, engineering standards, cloud strategy and long\-term platform scalability. The environment is heavily engineering\-led with a strong focus on Kubernetes at scale, Infrastructure as Code, observability, automation, platform reliability and modern DevOps practices across highly available production systems. **Non\-Negotiables** * Kubernetes in large\-scale production environments * DevOps or Platform Engineering experience across AWS, Azure and/or GCP * Terraform and Infrastructure as Code * Helm and Kubernetes package management * Strong Linux and containerisation experience * CI/CD pipelines and automation tooling * Kubernetes networking, security and observability * Experience operating highly available cloud platforms * Strong troubleshooting skills across Kubernetes and cloud environments * Experience working in complex enterprise or mission\-critical environments **What You’ll Work With** * Kubernetes * AWS / Azure / GCP * EKS / AKS / GKE * Terraform * Helm * GitOps workflows * ArgoCD / Flux * Prometheus * Grafana * Loki * Fluentd / Fluent Bit * RBAC and secrets management * Multi\-cloud and hybrid infrastructure * CI/CD and platform automation **Nice to Haves** * CKA / CKAD / CKS certifications * Service mesh technologies such as Istio, Linkerd or Cilium * Velero, Kasten or Kubernetes backup tooling * Spectro Cloud or enterprise Kubernetes platforms * SRE and reliability engineering experience * Experience mentoring engineers or setting technical standards * Open\-source Kubernetes or cloud\-native contributions * Experience supporting regulated or compliance\-heavy environments **Why Join** You’ll be joining during a significant investment into cloud\-native infrastructure and platform engineering, helping build and scale Kubernetes platforms that support critical global systems used across healthcare and other highly regulated industries. There’s strong technical ownership throughout the team. Senior engineers will influence automation, DevOps tooling and platform standards, while Principal\-level hires will shape architecture, cloud strategy and long\-term engineering direction across multi\-cloud environments. The Dublin engineering function is scaling rapidly, making this a strong opportunity for progression, visibility and the chance to help define how the DevOps and platform capability evolves over the coming years **DevOps Engineers \| Kubernetes, AWS, Azure \& GCP \| Multi\-Cloud Platform Build**

title: Document Controller
description: **About Us** Echelon Data Centres is an international data centre infrastructure developer providing large\-scale assets to support the growing global demand for data and computing resources – sustainably, responsibly and with due regard for the environment. **Job Summary** This role is responsible for operational documentation governance, compliance evidence management, controlled document lifecycle, and SharePoint repository oversight. The role ensures documentation integrity, regulatory alignment, and operational readiness support across the data centre and energy centre estate. **Key Responsibilities** **Operational Documentation Management:** * Manage controlled documentation (SOPs, EOPs, MOPs, policies). * Enforce metadata standards, versioning rules, approvals, and lifecycle governance. * Maintain master copies on SharePoint and archive superseded versions. **SharePoint Repository Governance** * Manage permissions, folder structures, metadata schemas, and file hygiene. * Conduct periodic audits on outdated, duplicate, and unclassified files. * Train teams on correct usage and governance compliance. **Compliance Management** * Maintain evidence for ISO 27001, ISO 45001, and statutory regulatory bodies. * Support audit preparation and provide structured evidence packs. * Track corrective actions and regulatory updates. **Collaboration** * Work with Operations, Engineering, EHS, IT, and vendors to support readiness. * Coordinate documentation inputs for OA/ORA programmes. * Facilitate cross\-team communication of documentation standards. **Reporting** * Produce monthly dashboards on documentation accuracy, compliance status, and repository hygiene. * Maintain reporting metrics for audits and readiness programs. **Qualifications** **Educational Background:** * Bachelor’s degree in business administration, compliance, IT, or equivalent field. **Professional Experience** * 3–5 years in document control, compliance, governance, or similar roles. * Experience in critical infrastructure or data centre environments preferred. **Certifications** * Desirable: ISO auditor, ITIL, SharePoint governance certifications. **Technical Skills** * Advanced SharePoint administration. * Strong knowledge of document control frameworks. * Understanding of ISO compliance structures. * Excellent analytical and organisational skills. **Personal Attributes** * Detail focused, structured, and methodical. * Strong communicator and stakeholder collaborator. * Self\-driven and capable of improving governance maturity. **Benefits** * Competitive salary and benefits. * Career development and progression. * Opportunity to shape governance standards across expanding facilities. All vacancies are managed directly by our Internal Team. Unsolicited contact or speculative CVs from unapproved agencies will not be accepted and no introductory fees will apply. *Echelon\-DC is an inclusive organisation, and we actively promote equality of opportunity for all with the right mix of talent, skills and potential. We welcome all applications from a wide range of candidates. Selection for roles will be based on individual merit alone. If you require reasonable accommodations to participate in the employee selection process, please contact People and Culture Team (* ***careers@echelon\-dc.com*** *) directly.*

title: Drug Product Associate, Sterile Drug Product
description: **Working with Us** Challenging. Meaningful. Life\-changing. Those aren't words that are usually associated with a job. But working at Bristol Myers Squibb is anything but usual. Here, uniquely interesting work happens every day, in every department. From optimizing a production line to the latest breakthroughs in cell therapy, this is work that transforms the lives of patients, and the careers of those who do it. You'll get the chance to grow and thrive through opportunities uncommon in scale and scope, alongside high\-achieving teams. Take your career farther than you thought possible. Bristol Myers Squibb recognizes the importance of balance and flexibility in our work environment. We offer a wide variety of competitive benefits, services and programs that provide our employees with the resources to pursue their goals, both at work and in their personal lives. Read more careers.bms.com/working\-with\-us . BMS Cruiserath Sterile Drug Product is seeking an experienced individual with manufacturing experience to provide on the floor technical support to our manufacturing teams within our new Sterile Drug Product (SDP) facility. Reporting to the Senior Manager, Manufacturing Shift Lead, the Drug Product Associate will play a key role in the establishing our multi\-product commercial Drug Product Facility with both vial and syringe fill finish technology. The **Drug Product Associate** will provide input and support all phases of the SDP project, such as, CQV, Site Operational Readiness, Technical Transfer, new product introduction and commercialization activities. Post start\-up this role will be embedded within the shift teams charged with delivering the manufacturing plan for the facility. The start\-up nature of this project will require adaptability \& innovation due to the multiple project phases and multiple products onsite. The role will be both challenging \& rewarding and will thus ensure that the successful candidate can seize a wide range of development opportunities within BMS. * The position will commence on a day schedule, with a planned transition to shift work in the future **Key Responsibilities** * Support the execution of one or more processes that include Formulation, Filling (vial \& syringe), Lyophilization, Capping, Visual Inspection \& Primary Packaging * Support technical issue troubleshooting, resolution both during and post facility start\-up. * Work with the cross functional departments to facilitate the implementation of the project design and validation of the SDP manufacturing facility. * Support all phases of Commissioning / Qualification and Validation during Start\-up phase of state\-of\-the\-art Drug Product Facility * Support all Capital / Tech Transfer / Operational Readiness activities to support Commercial Readiness * Support delivery of training to SDP team on day\-to\-day operations within area of responsibility * Right First\-Time execution of commercial manufacturing processes according to established work instructions * Support the implementation of all key Operational Excellence initiatives (5S / Std Work / LSW / Kaizen / Problem Solving etc.) * Taking part in investigations and optimization of processes using scientific, engineering and lean principles * Proficient use of process automation systems * Assisting with review of batch and exception reports for manufacturing lots in conjunction with Quality Assurance representative * Engages in and supports culture of continuous improvement initiatives and safe working practices. * Designee for Senior DPA as required. * Support any other business deliverables as needed. **Qualifications \& Experience** * Experience in a high\-speed manufacturing / regulated environment essential * Qualification in science, engineering, or related subject desirable. * Expertise in sterile drug product manufacturing along with demonstrated proficiency in technical troubleshooting and resolution is advantageous but not essential. * Ability to flexibly adapt to changing business needs in a start\-up environment. **Why you should apply** * You will help patients in their fight against serious diseases * You will be part of a company that encourages excellence and innovation, respects diversity, develops leaders and values its employees. * You'll get a competitive salary and a great benefits package including an annual bonus, pension contribution, family medical allowance, 27 days of annual leave, life assurance and on\-site gym If you come across a role that intrigues you but doesn't perfectly line up with your resume, we encourage you to apply anyway. You could be one step away from work that will transform your life and career. **Uniquely Interesting Work, Life\-changing Careers** With a single vision as inspiring as Transforming patients' lives through science™ , every BMS employee plays an integral role in work that goes far beyond ordinary. Each of us is empowered to apply our individual talents and unique perspectives in a supportive culture, promoting global participation in clinical trials, while our shared values of passion, innovation, urgency, accountability, inclusion and integrity bring out the highest potential of each of our colleagues. **On\-site Protocol** BMS has an occupancy structure that determines where an employee is required to conduct their work. This structure includes site\-essential, site\-by\-design, field\-based and remote\-by\-design jobs. The occupancy type that you are assigned is determined by the nature and responsibilities of your role Site\-essential roles require 100% of shifts onsite at your assigned facility. Site\-by\-design roles may be eligible for a hybrid work model with at least 50% onsite at your assigned facility. For these roles, onsite presence is considered an essential job function and is critical to collaboration, innovation, productivity, and a positive Company culture. For field\-based and remote\-by\-design roles the ability to physically travel to visit customers, patients or business partners and to attend meetings on behalf of BMS as directed is an essential job function. **Supporting People With Disabilities** BMS is dedicated to ensuring that people with disabilities can excel through a transparent recruitment process, reasonable workplace accommodations/adjustments and ongoing support in their roles. Applicants can request a reasonable workplace accommodation/adjustment prior to accepting a job offer. If you require reasonable accommodations/adjustments in completing this application, or in any part of the recruitment process, direct your inquiries to adastaffingsupport@bms.com . Visit careers.bms.com/ eeo \-accessibility to access our complete Equal Employment Opportunity statement. **Candidate Rights** BMS will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records, pursuant to applicable laws in your area. If you live in or expect to work from Los Angeles County if hired for this position, please visit this page for important additional information https //careers.bms.com/california\-residents/ **Data Protection** We will never request payments, financial information, or social security numbers during our application or recruitment process. Learn more about protecting yourself at https //careers.bms.com/fraud\-protection . Any data processed in connection with role applications will be treated in accordance with applicable data privacy policies and regulations. If you believe that the job posting is missing information required by local law or incorrect in any way, please contact BMS at TAEnablement@bms.com . Please provide the Job Title and Requisition number so we can review. Communications related to your application should not be sent to this email and you will not receive a response. Inquiries related to the status of your application should be directed to Chat with Ripley. R1602498 Drug Product Associate, Sterile Drug Product

title: EMEA Account Executive
description: **About LastPass** LastPass delivers Secure Access Essentials, helping individuals and organizations manage and protect access to AI, applications, and credentials straight from the browser. Trusted by more than 100,000 businesses and millions of users worldwide, LastPass blends strong security with everyday simplicity. From discovering unapproved AI and applications to reducing login friction and securing credentials across the business, LastPass helps teams and individuals stay productive, minimize risk, and remain prepared as their environments evolve. **Curious about our products? Visit our website and try it free!** **We welcome new ideas, support your growth, and recognize your value, if this aligns with what you are looking for in your next career move, Join Us!** **LastPass is looking for an EMEA Account Executive:** In this role, your focus will be on building new business as well as driving upsell and cross‑sell opportunities across our customer base. You will be set up for success with the support of Channel partners, Product Specialists, Solutions Consulting, and Business Development Representatives. *This role follows a hybrid work model and requires working onsite in our Dublin office 1 day per week.* **About the Team:** When you join our sales team, you’re helping show customers that their world can be just as accessible as it is secure. You’ll be educating and empowering businesses to move confidently and securely through an always‑on world \- keeping their data safe and protected with less effort and greater peace of mind. **If you are passionate about complex problem solving and motivated by scale, then this is the role for you!** **Who will you work with?** You’ll work toward a shared goal with an open‑minded, collaborative team that truly thrives as the sum of its parts. **What are some of the exciting challenges you will be working on?** * Helping LastPass restore revenue growth. We are a creative group focused on continuously refining our sales process to achieve our FY targets * Enhancing our partner motion * Obsessing over value creation for customers * Maintaining a strong local focus within your territory, while also developing global awareness of industry news and trends **What does it take to work at LastPass?** * Proven success in a high‑velocity, quota‑carrying role * Strong pipeline management skills, with the ability to forecast consistently and accurately * A self‑starter mindset, paired with strong time management and accountability * Experience owning and managing a territory with a strategic approach to account mapping * A solid understanding of partner and channel programs, and the ability to build and leverage those relationships effectively * The ability to use sales dashboards and reports to efficiently manage and own your territory * A competitive drive, combined with a continuous pursuit of growth and self‑improvement * A genuine desire to bring value to customers and support teammates in achieving shared goals * A positive, solution‑oriented mindset focused on problem‑solving and advancing our mission **It's great, but not required:** * The ability to travel to partner or company events * Experience customizing CRM reports to work efficiently with territory data * Previous experience with Clari, our internal forecasting tool * Experience leveraging AI tools in a sales workflow (e.g., for research, prospecting, data analysis, messaging, or productivity improvements) **Why LastPass?** * Market\-leading password manager * High\-growth, collaborative environment with inclusive teams * Remote\-first culture * Competitive compensation * Flexible Paid Time Off policies, including but not limited to: Quarterly Self\-Care Days (4 extra paid days off annually) and Volunteer Days * Parental leave * Comprehensive health coverage, including dependents * Home office setup support * LastPass Families free account for up to 5 members * Continuous learning and development opportunities, including an annual learning stipend to invest in your growth * Peer\-to\-peer recognition through Motivosity * Employee Assistance Program for well\-being support * Remote work stipend to support your home office needs * Short\-Term or Remote\-Centric Work Arrangements for added flexibility ***Unlock your potential with us \- your skills, experience, and unique perspective matter more than just checking the boxes. Apply today, and let's build the future together!*** We’re building an inclusive community that reflects the people of all races, genders, sexual orientations, national origins, backgrounds, and perspectives who share our world. For all US based jobs please review our Applicant Privacy Notice For all EU based jobs please review our Candidate Privacy Notice Please review our CCPA Notice

title: ESG Risk Officer - Risk Advisory
description: Location Dublin Business Area Legal, Compliance, and Risk Ref \# 10050189 **Description \& Requirements** The energy of a newsroom, the pace of a trading floor, the buzz of a recent tech breakthrough; we work hard, and we work fast \- while keeping up the quality and accuracy we're known for. It's what keeps us inventing and reinventing, all the time. Our culture is wide open, just like our spaces. We bring out the best in each other through collaboration. Through our countless volunteer projects, we also help network with the communities around us. You can do amazing work here. Work you couldn't do anywhere else. It's up to you to make it happen. **What's the role?** Bloomberg's Chief Risk Office (CRO) Department plays a critical role in supporting our businesses and operations around the world. We move quickly and thoughtfully to help address risks that are inherent in being the world's leading financial news and information company. The CRO team provides coverage of both Bloomberg's products and services, globally. Our focus is to address risks and related issues by providing risk management as a service, focusing on delivering value to help businesses and departments thoughtfully incorporate risk into decision\-making. The Risk Advisory Team Within CRO Is Seeking An Experienced Risk Officer To Support Bloomberg’s ESG Ratings Offering. The Risk Officer Has Responsibility For * Ensuring the Governance, Risk and Control Frameworks are operating effectively; * Maintaining and updating such frameworks as approved by the relevant Board; * Measuring the Risk Frameworks against agreed risk appetite and tolerance levels; * Compiling, assessing and reporting risk information to the relevant Board; * Partnering with Compliance to ensure control and process activities comply with relevant regulations. **We Will Trust You To** * Support the development and ongoing enhancement of a comprehensive risk management framework for ESG rating activities * Develop, manage and refine qualitative and quantitative risk reporting which meets the needs of the relevant Board’s effective decision\-making * Work closely with the relevant Board and senior managers across the business in shaping a high\-achieving, well\-controlled organisation * Formulate and articulate risk appetite and tolerance thresholds that can be capitalised using financial models * Partner with Business and Technology to perform risk and controls identification, deliver efficiency and control improvements, where necessary, through process analysis, collaboration, design, and implementation * Implement and oversee an issue management process, including issue identification, root cause analysis, remediation tracking, and documentation * Analyse functional and support gaps, identify risks, propose solutions, and manage execution * Provide second\-line oversight, review, and challenge of ESG rating models and methodologies * Assess the risk implications of methodology or model changes and provide independent challenge to ensure sound governance and decision\-making * Partner effectively with third parties, regulatory bodies and others, as appropriate, and serve as a role model for high personal and corporate ethical values and standards of integrity * Maintain subject matter expertise on evolving ESG regulatory requirements, with a focus on those affecting ESG ratings and model governance **You Will Need To Have** * 10\+ years\* of experience in risk management, data management, technology, corporate governance, regulatory and operational risk, or information and security risk * Bachelor's degree or higher in Finance, Economics, Information Systems, or related discipline * Recognised risk certification (i.e. PRM or FRM) preferred * Strong understanding of fundamentals of risk management, control assessment and/or control testing activities * Demonstrated ability to influence and advise senior stakeholders across different teams * Knowledge of the regulatory environment and key regulatory regimes, including basic understanding of EU and UK regulatory landscapes * Ability to communicate complex regulatory, model, and risk concepts clearly to both technical and non\-technical audiences * Proven intellectual curiosity through professional training, advanced degree or professional impact * Prior experience in either a 1st or 2nd line of defence capacity * Excellent time management skills and ability to deliver work on assigned schedules * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills to act decisively and independently to resolve sophisticated issues **We’d Love To See** * Individuals who are passionate about all things Risk and are genuinely excited about playing their part in helping build a best\-in\-class program * Self\-starter, with the ability to work independently with minimal guidance and a positive work attitude * Strong business acumen * Familiarity with ratings\-related regulatory requirements and/or control practices * Experience directly engaging with regulators * Please note we use years of experience as a guide, but we certainly will consider applications from all candidates who are able to demonstrate the skills necessary for the role. If indicated, please note that years of experience are a guide; we will consider applications from all candidates who can demonstrate the skills necessary for the role. Discover what makes Bloomberg unique \- watch our for an inside look at our culture, values, and the people behind our success.

title: Electrical BIM Coordinator
description: **Electrical BIM Coordinator \| Dublin (Hybrid) \| 12\-Month Contract** **€50–€60 per hour** We’re looking for an experienced **Electrical BIM Coordinator** to take ownership of electrical BIM delivery on large\-scale, regulated engineering projects in Dublin. This role focuses on producing **fully coordinated, construction\-ready Revit models** and driving multidisciplinary integration across complex project environments. You’ll play a key role in ensuring design accuracy, clash\-free coordination, and high\-quality BIM deliverables from design through to commissioning. **Key Responsibilities** * Lead electrical BIM model development in Revit (power, lighting, containment, earthing, layouts) * Deliver coordinated, construction\-ready drawings across all project phases * Manage clash detection and resolution (Navisworks or similar) * Coordinate closely with MEP, structural, architectural \& automation teams * Translate electrical design data (SLDs, load schedules, vendor info) into BIM outputs * Maintain model quality, standards, and data integrity * Support coordination meetings and drive issue resolution * Mentor junior BIM staff and promote best practice **What you need** * Strong Electrical BIM coordination experience in Revit * Background in industrial/pharma or regulated environments * Solid understanding of electrical systems design * Experience with Navisworks or clash detection tools * Ability to read and interpret electrical engineering documentation * Strong coordination and communication skills across disciplines Candidates must hold a valid Irish work visa or EU passport. 📞 Contact Corné: 094 931 6002

title: Electrical Technician
description: **Req ID:** 502685 **Location: Dublin** **Appointment Basis: Permanent** **Apply by: 28/05/2026** **Salary: circa** **€50,000 \-** **€60,000 DOE plus shift allowance** At Alstom, we understand transport networks and what moves people. From high\-speed trains, metros, monorails, and trams, to turnkey systems, services, infrastructure, signalling and digital mobility, we offer our diverse customers the broadest portfolio in the industry. Every day, 80,000 colleagues lead the way to greener and smarter mobility worldwide, connecting cities as we reduce carbon and replace cars. Alstom is the UK \& Ireland’s leading supplier of new trains and train services, and a leading signalling and rail infrastructure provider. Alstom have built, or are building, just under 40% of the UK mainline train fleet; as well as the entire fleets in service with London Underground and Dublin Luas. We provide the widest range of smart solutions in the rail market, from innovative high\-speed rolling stock, metros and trams to maintenance, modernisation, infrastructure and signalling. **Your future role** Take on a new challenge and apply your Electrical systems knowledge as a technical team member in a new cutting\-edge field. You’ll report to Train manager and work alongside our active and highly resourceful colleagues. You’ll be able to demonstrate your unique attributes and skills. Day\-to\-day, you’ll work closely with teams across the business, carry out testing and commissioning activities on dart\+ trains in accordance with pre\-written test and commissioning procedures and much more. You’ll specifically take care of all coupling activities, static and dynamic testing and warranty issues and modifications. We’ll look to you for: * Develop and increase knowledge of all Dart\+ train functions and sub\-systems. * Troubleshooting of all warranty defects and provide technical assistance on TSSSA contract. * Upload software and downloads to carry out critical asset repairs and upgrades. * Apply special processes as crimping and torque tightening. * Functionally test train borne systems with minimal supervision using a variety of techniques and methods, including the use of computer\-based diagnostics systems. * Provide technical support to the depot teams in the provision of fault\-finding expertise and assistance with train repair and train maintenance. * Undertake test runs to assist in fault finding and resolution of faults, in particular the close\-out of repeat failures. * Assistance in component change outs, and failure reporting for ESG and reliability growth teams * Liaise with the ESG and specialist engineers as necessary, gathering technical information needed to enable technical problems with trains, procedures, materials or component problems to be resolved. * Carry out all duties in accordance with the Traincare Centre quality and safety procedures. * Driving Company vehicles for Depot operational or Technical related work such as breakdowns or occasional travel to do other work at other sites, this would only be requested of current eligible and authorised Technicians as agreed with relevant site TCM \& HRM. * Carrying out overhead isolations as required. * Topping up of sanding systems of rail vehicles * General depot duties e.g.: Line of Routes * Static / functional and performance tests * Review and rectify test specifications and documentation for functional tests. * Continuously improve processes and procedures. * Coach and develop other Technicians as required. * To record all Service Orders all activities and time taken to complete tasks. * Attend regular team briefings and communicate any relevant matters. * Report accidents and incidents through the appropriate channels **All about you** We value passion and attitude over experience. That’s why we don’t expect you to have every single skill. Instead, we’ve listed some that we think will help you succeed and grow in this role: * Qualified in a Technical Discipline – Qualified Electrician FETAC Level 6 National Craft Certificate (NCC) is required for this role * Experience of electronics, hydraulics, pneumatics and mechanics. * Experience of working within a process / production environment. * Understanding work instructions and following procedures. * Ability to read detailed electrical drawings and cable schedules. * Applying electrical modifications * To inform the files of progress of modifications * Ability to fault find/diagnose issues on installations while testing. * Be adaptable and flexible. * Working knowledge of quality and health and safety standards and systems preferable * Crimping and Torque Tightening. * Efficient in Mechanical and electrical coupling. * Uploading of software. * IT literate with some knowledge of Microsoft software packages. * Good organisational skills. **Things you’ll enjoy** Join us on a life\-long transformative journey – the rail industry is here to stay, so you can grow and develop new skills and experiences throughout your career. You’ll also: * Enjoy stability, challenges and a long\-term career free from boring daily routines * Work with Alstom to deliver innovative and sustainable transport systems. * Collaborate with transverse teams and helpful colleagues * Contribute to innovative projects * Utilise Alstom’s essential values \- Agile, Inclusive and Responsible. These values are the driving force for Alstom employees in their working lives working environment. * Steer your career in whatever direction you choose across functions and countries * Benefit from our investment in your development, through award\-winning learning * Advancement in career progression and career growth * Benefit from a fair and dynamic reward package that recognises your performance and potential, plus comprehensive and competitive social coverage (life, medical, pension) You don’t need to be a train enthusiast to thrive with us. We guarantee that when you step onto one of our trains with your friends or family, you’ll be proud. If you’re up for the challenge, we’d love to hear from you! **What we offer** We offer a highly competitive remuneration package, days annual leave, plus bank holidays, with the opportunity to buy or sell holiday. Contributed healthcare, life assurance, an excellent company\-contributed pension scheme, and a wide range of flexible benefits that you can tailor to suit your lifestyle. Alstom is the leading company in the mobility sector, solving the most interesting challenges for tomorrow’s mobility. That’s why we value inquisitive and innovative people who are passionate about working together to reinvent mobility, making it smarter and more sustainable. Day after day, we are building an agile, inclusive, and responsible culture, where a diverse group of people are offered opportunities to learn, grow and advance in their careers, with options across functions and geographic locations. Are you ready to join a truly international community of great people on a challenging journey with a tangible impact and purpose? **Equal opportunity statement:** Alstom is an equal opportunity employer committed to creating an inclusive working environment where all our employees are encouraged to reach their full potential, and individual differences are valued and respected. All qualified applicants are considered for employment without regard to race, colour, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, national origin, disability status, or any other characteristic protected by local law. As a 'Disability Confident' employer, we will interview all disabled job applicants who match the essential criteria of the job description or specification. We will consider flexible working requests for all roles unless operational requirements prevent otherwise. \#LI\-AL2 **Job Segment:** Testing, Technician, Bank, Banking, Technical Support, Technology, Finance

title: Electrician (Shift )
description: **Role Purpose** CBRE Global Workplace Solutions, leading global provider of integrated facilities and corporate real estate management, are recruiting an Electrical maintenance technician to join the team located in Dublin 1\. As a CBRE Critical Services Technician, you will resolve and complete day\-to\-day work orders to meet customer satisfaction targets and ensure completion in the time frame required. This job is part of the Critical and Technical Services job function. They are responsible for technical maintenance services in critical client environments. **Primary Objectives And Tasks** This Role is a rotational day shift position on site, covering 6 over 7 days Monday \-Saturday 7am \- 7pm Week 1 \= 4 days Mon\-Thurs. Week 2 Thurs\-Sat \= 3 days * Electrical maintenance \& Utilities maintenance Technician Onsite. * Qualify and act as an appointed Authorized Person (AP) within 6 months from onboarding. * After hours On call roster participation is required for this position with transport and allowance available during your week on call. * Be proficient with onsite asset management system (REALM). * Work closely with the Permit / Technical Administrator. * Responsible for correct operation of equipment and on time PPM maintenance tasks performed on site equipment relating to your technical ability. * Oversee Vendor service and repair visits as point of contact and ensuring tasks are completed to CBRE standards. Review reports and highlight any issues for escalation and scheduled repairs. * Ensure that comprehensive maintenance records are kept in the form of “Frontline\\" task sheets and update Log books associated with Electrical \& HVAC systems. * Ensure that Method Statements and Risk Assessments are prepared for all tasks carried out to ensure safe working practices. * To develop a good working relationship with all members of CBRE Managed Services staff and clients staff. * Hands on service and repairs works with a view to mentoring junior technicians and non\-technical staff. * Work closely with Facilities team in dealing with Client requests / work orders for timely close out and resolution of issues relating to Electrical \& HVAC systems. * Ensure a professional image of CBRE Managed Services is presented to clients and visitors, and ensure excellence in customer service is delivered and promoted at all times. * Ensure that professional and technical skills are maintained through a commitment to personal development and life\-long\-learning. * A flexible approach is required for the consistent and high standard of service CBRE provide. * Flexibility should the need arise to alter start and finish times and to participate with shift and holiday cover as per the clients needs to meet contractual obligations. * At times technical upskill will be required for on\-site equipment such as BMS, fire alarm lighting controls and various equipment operational systems and devices. **Accountabilities** * Accountable to Contract Engineering Manager. * Operate as system champion for assigned responsible equipment within your scope and understanding. * The post holder does not have any directly reporting staff. * This post carries no direct budgetary responsibility **Core Competencies** * Trade Qualified Electrician , must have good written and spoken English and basic P.C skills. * Formally trained in Electrical installation and maintenance systems. * M.V \& L.V switching and operational understanding. Electrical AP appointed CBRE standard. * M.V \& L.V Training \& certification required. ( training can be provided for the right candidate). * Operational experience of building management control systems. ( required ). * Electrical testing, fixed wire, appliance and PAT. ( distinct advantage ). * Formal health and safety understanding \- training (Desirable) **Experience** * Proficient with electrical installation and maintenance standards. * Vendor and Contractor management experience, also team lead experience desirable. * Experience of maintaining Pumps, Motors AHU’s FCU’s heating and cooling systems. * Experience of generator \& UPS systems for 100% uptime available of comms rooms. * Critical utilities, Electrical, A/C and dual power supplies will be a distinct advantage. * Experience of operating and maintenance CMMS \& BMS, fire alarm and lighting system. * Sound understanding and experience for meeting health and safety obligations with regard to maintenance activities. (Desirable) * Working knowledge of statutory and regulatory equipment and testing requirements. * Sound understanding of HVAC and building services systems. * Some project management experience or team lead experience desirable. **Aptitudes** * Good verbal communication skills * Good written English skills * Ability to read write and understand operating manuals, SOP’s MOP’S and detailed reports. * Working knowledge of Microsoft Office products. Examples include Word, Excel, Outlook, etc. **Character** * Committed to the delivery of excellent customer service. * Calm manner, able to work under pressure. * Able to make sound decisions when needed. * Meet the physical requirements of this role including stooping, standing, walking, climbing stairs/ladders, and the ability to lift/carry heavy loads of 50 lbs. or more. * Able to ascend and descend vertical access equipment. * Able to work at heights including MEWP, ladders \& mobile towers etc. * A team player, able to work with CBRE Managed Services and clients staff of all levels. * Strong organizational skills with an inquisitive mindset **Circumstances** * Full Clean Drivers License for use of Company Van or access to own Vehicle For OOH callout’ * Able to attend the site location within one hour of notification, for on\-site attendance while participating in out\-of\-hours rostered stand\-by and call\-out procedures.

title: Engineer III, Cloud
description: As a global leader in cybersecurity, CrowdStrike protects the people, processes and technologies that drive modern organizations. Since 2011, our mission hasn’t changed — we’re here to stop breaches, and we’ve redefined modern security with the world’s most advanced AI\-native platform. We work on large scale distributed systems, processing almost 3 trillion events per day and this traffic is growing daily. Our customers span all industries, and they count on CrowdStrike to keep their businesses running, their communities safe and their lives moving forward. We’re also a mission\-driven company. We cultivate a culture that gives every CrowdStriker both the flexibility and autonomy to own their careers. We’re always looking to add talented CrowdStrikers to the team who have limitless passion, a relentless focus on innovation and a fanatical commitment to our customers, our community and each other. Ready to join a mission that matters? The future of cybersecurity starts with you. ******About The Role :****** We’re building the next\-generation cloud\-native security platform for CrowdStrike which includes an application and service delivery platform, massively scalable distributed data storage and replication systems, and a cutting\-edge search and distributed graph system. You will play a critical role in innovating and building the CrowdStrike Asset Graph system. Bring your experience in distributed technologies and algorithms, your great API and systems design sensibilities, and your passion for writing code that performs at extreme scale. If you want a job that makes a difference in the world and operates at a high scale, you’ve come to the right place. This is a hybrid position based in Dublin. ******What You'll Do:****** * Lead backend engineering efforts from rapid prototypes to large\-scale applications across CrowdStrike products. * Design, develop, document, test, deploy, maintain, and enhance large scale services. * Take ownership and be accountable for deliverables. * Triage system issues and debug by analyzing the sources of issues and the impact on service operations. * Mentor other engineers on web, backend and data storage technologies and our system. * Constantly re\-evaluate our product to improve architecture, knowledge models, user experience, performance and stability. * Partner with product teams in building their use\-cases and solutions on the Asset Graph platform ******What You'll Use:****** * Go(Golang) * title: Engineer QA, PC Apps
description: Join the leader in entertainment innovation and help us design the future. At Dolby, science meets art, and high tech means more than computer code. As a member of the Dolby team, you’ll see and hear the results of your work everywhere, from movie theaters to smartphones. We continue to revolutionize how people create, deliver, and enjoy entertainment worldwide. To do that, we need the absolute best talent. We’re big enough to give you all the resources you need, and small enough so you can make a real difference and earn recognition for your work. We offer a collegial culture, challenging projects, and excellent compensation and benefits, not to mention a Flex Work approach that is truly flexible to support where, when, and how you do your best work. **The role** For Dolby’s PC Applications, you will support the quality, stability, and reliability of consumer\-facing client applications used by end users at scale. As an Engineer QA, you will primarily focus on manual and exploratory testing, supporting feature development and release validation while gradually building ownership of assigned test areas. This role reflects the traditional QA Engineer role used on Dolby Access and is intentionally suitable for manual QA engineers, with optional paths toward automation over time. **What We Offer** * Work on consumer\-facing PC applications with visible impact on real users. * A collaborative QA and engineering environment focused on quality, learning, and shared ownership. * Stable products, clear expectations, and realistic scope for manual quality assurance. * Opportunities to grow toward automation or senior SQA roles over time. **What You Will Do** * Execute manual and exploratory tests for assigned features or application areas. * Design, execute, and maintain test cases based on requirements and specifications. * Participate in release validation, regression testing, and smoke testing activities. * Clearly report defects and work with software engineers to support investigation and resolution. * Maintain basic test documentation and contribute to improving test coverage over time. **Required** **What you bring** * Experience in manual software testing or quality assurance for consumer or client\-side applications. * Good understanding of software testing fundamentals, including test case design, execution, and defect reporting. * Ability to follow test plans, document results, and work in a structured manner. * Attention to detail and a quality\-focused mindset. * Good communication skills and ability to collaborate effectively within a team. **Nice to have** * Experience testing Windows\-based desktop applications. * Experience with test automation or scripting (e.g. Python. * Familiarity with bug tracking tools and basic CI or release validation workflows. * Interest in growing toward automation or advanced SQA roles in the future. Dolby Hiring Entity: 77 Sir John Rogerson's Quay, Block C Grand Canal Docklands Dublin D02 VK60Ireland

title: Engineering Apprentice (Polymer)
description: **Description** At West, we’re a dedicated team that is connected by a purpose to improve patient lives that has been at the center of our Company for more than a century. Our story began when Herman O. West solved the problem of supplying penicillin in mass quantities to the US Government during World War 2\. Through our work to deliver thousands of life\-saving and life\-enhancing injectable medicines to millions of patients daily, West’s indelible mark on the healthcare industry has just begun. A name started our story. How will yours help write our future? There’s no better place to join an inclusive community of professionals with opportunities for lifelong learning, growth and development. Supported by benefit programs, we empower the physical, mental, emotional and financial health of our team members and their families. We believe in giving back to help those in need in the communities where we live and work. And are equally committed to creating a healthier environment and planet through our sustainability efforts. **Job Summary** The Polymer Apprentice will be responsible for the efficient set up and operation of polymer processing lines in the fields of injection moulding for the production of plastic components relevant to industry standards. Responsible for operating moulding and any auxiliary equipment. Responsible for inspecting product to a pre\-determined specification and completing all documentation necessary to provide quality, production and traceability records in accordance with ISO9002/EN46002/ISO14001 standards/ customer specifications. Responsible for making technical adjustments to the machine as required. Reports to the Moulding Supervisor. **Essential Duties And Responsibilities** * Take part in phased college semesters in accordance with course requirements and ensure course work and exams are completed and passed. * Ensures the quality of the parts produced by inspecting parts visually and through measuring devices. * Supporting the shift and Shift Supervisor by reporting on/resolving process/technical issues. * Ensure the machine settings as per the validated status are recorded on relevant documentation if applicable. * Attend production meetings as required. * Attend to all incidents of downtime and record in the relevant documentation on a daily basis. * Define machine status on OEE System in order to determine machine downtime and run time. * Assisting with the introduction and validation of new tooling as required. * Work closely with maintenance, tool room and process engineers to ensure technical support for the room. * Completes Tool changes. * Provide assistance, support, and training to operators on the shift. * Performs line clearance in conjunction with the relevant procedures. * Maintaining accuracy of batch documentation at all times and ensuring that it is filled in and right first time. * Ensure that machines are stopped and started as per the relevant start up procedures in manufacturing manual. * Understands and safely operates all equipment. * Reports and/or corrects unsafe working conditions. * Ensures all moulding machines and ancillary equipment are working correctly and reports any problems. * Performs mold cleaning and cleaning of machines/robots and ancillary equipment on a daily/monthly basis. * Ensures that the correct raw materials are being used at each moulding machine; ensures raw material is staged/ready for next shift. * Maintains work areas in a clean and orderly manner by practicing good housekeeping. * Maintains and updates assigned measures of performance, in conjunction with the Production Group leader. * Compliance to all site Environmental, Health and Safety requirements training, and regulations. * Compliance to all local site company policies, procedures and corporate policies. * Act in accordance with the company’s guiding principles and adherence to the corporate Code of Conduct. * Develops and maintains methods/procedures and all supporting documentation to meet the requirements of ISO 9001/ ISO 13485 / ISO 14001\. * Performs additional duties at the request of the direct supervisor. ****Education**** * Leaving Certificate or equivalent, Grade 06 at ordinary level in 5 subjects, 2 of which must be math’s and a language (English or Irish) **Basic Qualifications** * Willing to travel and temporary relocation to complete educational modules. * Flexibility to adapt to required working pattern which will include days and shift. * Must have good verbal reasoning, numerate ability and mechanical aptitude skills. * Must have good communication skills, both written and oral. * Must be willing to work on a shift basis in the business. **Preferred Knowledge, Skills And Abilities** * Previous experience working in a team environment. * Ability to work on their own initiative. * Previous production work experience in an injection\-moulding environment would be an advantage. ****Travel Requirements**** 10%: Up to 26 business days per year ****Physical Requirements**** Medium\-Exerting up to 50lb/22kg of force occasionally and/or up to 20lbs/9kg of force frequently, and/or up to 10lbs/4kgs of force constantly to move objects. **Mental Requirements** Must be able to work in a fast\-paced environment. Must be able to organize and prioritize tasks; must be detail orientated and self\-motivated. Must be able to deal with all levels of employees and work across various departments. Must be able to work independently on own projects while working concurrently with all departments. Establishes relationships with the customers and Equipment/ Raw Material suppliers in pursuit of continuous improvement and on\-going business development. West is an equal opportunity employer and we value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. If you have a special need that requires accommodation in order to apply to West, please send an email to Apply.Accommodation@westpharma.com . Where permitted by law, an offer of employment with West Pharmaceutical Services, or any of its subsidiary or affiliate companies, is contingent upon the satisfactory completion of background screening.

title: Entry Level - Site Reliability Engineer (Remote - Ireland)
description: **Summary** Yelp engineering culture is driven by our values: we’re a cooperative team that values individual authenticity and encourages creative solutions to problems. All new engineers deploy working code their first week, and we strive to broaden individual impact with support from managers, mentors, and teams. At the end of the day, we’re all about helping our users, growing as engineers, and having fun in a collaborative environment. Do you want to build and manage scaleable, self\-healing, globally\-distributed systems? Our Site Reliability engineers keep Yelp fast, available, and growing, connecting users to great local businesses. No matter how many times we get searched, scraped, scanned, spammed, pinged, paged, or queried, we keep our cool \- and keep the site running smoothly. We work in both the dev and systems worlds, implementing key parts of the core architecture and supporting devs as they try to do the same. We get to tackle interesting challenges that you can only find at the kind of scale that serves over 100 million users per month. You'll work to empower product teams and developers: at Yelp, spinning up infrastructure should always be a git commit and a code review away: automation and self\-service are at the core of what we do. This opportunity requires you to be located in the Republic of Ireland. We’d love to have you apply, even if you don’t feel you meet every single requirement in this posting. At Yelp, we’re looking for great people, not just those who simply check off all the boxes. **What You'll Do** * Work closely with engineers in supporting new features and services. * Build tools to monitor site stability and performance. * Help ensure the reliability and scalability of our infrastructure, while maintaining platform SLOs. * Troubleshoot site issues using industry\-leading tools like Splunk, Prometheus and OpenTelemetry. * Automate everything with Python, Puppet, Git, Jenkins, and Terraform and leveraging an extensive suite of AI tooling. * Develop custom tools when off\-the\-shelf solutions don’t work at our scale and contribute upstream to open source projects. * Participate in light on\-call rotations \- we have geographically distributed SRE teams for follow\-the\-sun support, which means nobody needs to be on\-call 24h a day! **What It Takes To Succeed** * Familiarity with Linux and an enthusiasm for learning more (we use Ubuntu but any distro is fine). * Command of your favorite modern programming language: Python, Ruby, Go, Java, C\+\+, etc. * Hands\-on experience with public cloud platforms (we use AWS, but Azure/GCP are fine). * Practical background in Infrastructure as Code and Configuration Management (e.g., Terraform, Puppet, Ansible, or similar). * Relevant experience with Linux containerisation and orchestration (e.g., Docker and Kubernetes). * A solid understanding of fundamental technologies like TCP/IP, HTTP (transport, serving and load\-balancing), and DNS, containerization \& workload orchestration or datastores and streaming technologies. * Exposure to monitoring concepts and best practices around performance, reliability and security. * Excellent communication and documentation skills. * An eagerness to ask questions, take initiative and learn everyday. **What You'll Get** * Full responsibility for projects from day one, a collaborative team, and a dynamic work environment. * Competitive salary, a pension scheme, and an optional employee stock purchase plan. * 25 days paid holiday (rising to 29 with service), plus one floating holiday. * €150 monthly reimbursement to help cover remote working expenses. * €86 caregiver reimbursement to support dependent care for families. * Private health insurance, including dental and vision. * Flexible working hours and meeting\-free Wednesdays. * Regular 3\-day Hackathons, bi\-weekly learning groups, and productivity spending to support and encourage your career growth. * Opportunities to participate in digital events and conferences. * €86 per month to use toward qualifying wellness expenses. * Quarterly team offsites. Closing *Yelp values diversity. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and consider qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, ancestry, age, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital or family status, veteran status, medical condition, disability, or any other protected status.* **Notice to Northern Ireland Applicants** *A Basic criminal background check via AccessNI is required for employment. Yelp complies with the AccessNI Code of Practice. Having a criminal record will not necessarily prevent a candidate from working with Yelp. Yelp will consider the nature of the position together with the circumstances and background of the candidate's offences or other information contained on a disclosure certificate. AccessNI's Privacy Policy is available here. Yelp's Criminal Background Check Policy is available upon request.* *Note: Yelp does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to any recruiting alias or employee. Yelp is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.* Recruiting and Applicant Privacy Notice

description: **About Tangent** Tangent was created to make job applications fairer and more human. We connect you with industry expert mentors from leading companies who volunteer their time to guide you through every step of your job search. Our mentors help you improve your CV, prepare for interviews, and navigate salary negotiations with confidence. In today’s market, having a mentor can be the difference between being overlooked and getting hired. They’re now expanding their **Go\-To\-Market team** and are looking for ambitious individuals who are eager to learn, grow, and make a real impact in a fast\-paced environment. **The Role** As a **Business Development Representative (BDR)** , you’ll play a key role in driving the company’s growth by identifying new opportunities, building relationships, and generating leads for the wider sales team.
