title: Graduate Innovation Analyst
description: **Take Your Career to New Heights with ASL Aviation Holdings** Join ASL Aviation Holdings, a global aviation group with over 150 aircraft and 4,000 people across 51 nationalities. Whether on the ground or in the air, we offer exciting opportunities to grow, learn, and shape your career. **Who We Are** Headquartered in Dublin, Ireland, ASL operates five wholly owned airlines across Europe and Australia, and joint ventures in South Africa, Thailand, and India. Our airlines deliver cargo for the world’s leading parcel integrators and online retailers and provide passenger and charter services worldwide. We also operate ASL Maintenance, a certified EASA Part 145 organisation keeping our fleet of B747s and B737s flying safely. **Why ASL?** * Career Development: Clear growth paths, learning, and advancement opportunities. * Inclusive Culture: Gender diversity initiatives and fair employment practices across all airlines. * Global Experience: Work with a diverse, international team in a fast\-paced, dynamic industry. * Job Satisfaction: Be part of a workplace that motivates, inspires, and rewards your contribution. At ASL, you don’t just start a job, you start a career in aviation, with the support, opportunities, and expertise to help you reach new heights. *ASL Aviation Holdings \- where your career takes flight.* **Position:** ------------- Drive business improvement at ASL by applying AI\-powered analysis and software development across operational business areas. Using Claude as both an advanced analytical tool and a development platform, the Graduate Innovation Analyst will help the business optimise processes, streamline data flows, and deploy innovative solutions that align with ASL's strategic goals. **KEY ACCOUNTABILITIES** **Stakeholder Engagement:** Act as the intermediary between business areas and the Innovation Team. Engage stakeholders across Operations, Maintenance, Engineering, Supply Chain Shared Service and other business areas. Facilitate effective communication and provide regular progress and status reporting to all stakeholders. **AI\-Powered Analysis:** Use Claude as an advanced analytical tool to interrogate business data, synthesise operational documents, map processes, and surface insights that drive business understanding and decision\-making. **Solution Development:** Design, build, test, and deploy Claude\-powered applications that solve real operational problems. Move between business analyst work and developer work as each project requires. **Project Delivery:** Follow a lean project methodology to coordinate project activities through feasibility, initiation, planning, execution, monitoring and control, and closure. Perform quality control on solutions, manage scope, risks and decisions, and ensure change management is built into every delivery. **Process Optimisation:** Apply a standardised lean approach to business optimisation: define the problem and scope, measure current process performance, analyse cause and effect to identify root causes, and improve and control the process. Use BPMN 2\.0 for process mapping where appropriate. **Innovative Thinking:** Identify high\-impact business optimisation opportunities. Design and innovate solutions that are smart, scalable, cost\-effective and maintainable, with the purpose of increasing productivity, eliminating waste, saving time and reducing cost. **Technical and Domain Capability:** Build proficiency in operational systems relevant to assigned projects, including ASL's flight operations (CAE), MRO (Swiss\-AS), and Google Cloud Platform environments. Develop specialised knowledge of designated business areas with the aim of becoming a subject matter expert. **Adherence to Standards:** Maintain project tasks, milestones, statuses and risks on standardised platforms. Close out project documentation on standardised knowledge repositories. Maintain the process repository and keep process documentation up to date. **Requirements:** ----------------- **Qualifications:** First\-class or 2\.1 honours degree in Business Information Systems (BIS) or equivalent. A strong academic record is the baseline expectation. **Experience:** Graduate entry role. No prior full\-time experience required. Evidence of self\-directed learning, personal projects, internship delivery, or side work that demonstrates initiative is a strong signal. Familiarity with at least one programming language (Python, JavaScript or similar) is expected. Genuine interest in AI and the Claude platform is essential. **Other information:** ---------------------- Based in our group office in Swords Dublin

title: Graduate Program - Process & Operations Transformation
description: This role has been designed as ‘Hybrid’ with an expectation that you will work on average 2 days per week from an HPE office. **Who We Are** Hewlett Packard Enterprise is the global edge\-to\-cloud company advancing the way people live and work. We help companies connect, protect, analyze, and act on their data and applications wherever they live, from edge to cloud, so they can turn insights into outcomes at the speed required to thrive in today’s complex world. Our culture thrives on finding new and better ways to accelerate what’s next. We know varied backgrounds are valued and succeed here. We have the flexibility to manage our work and personal needs. We make bold moves, together, and are a force for good. If you are looking to stretch and grow your career our culture will embrace you. Open up opportunities with HPE. **Job Description** **About the Program** As a rotational Graduate in our **PREP (Professional Readiness \& Excellence Program)** within the **Office of the Chief Operating Officer (OCOO),** you will work on strategic, operational, and transformational initiatives across multiple OCOO teams for **12\-24 months in Ireland** . **Starting in September 2026,** you will gain hands\-on experience in service excellence, operations transformation, digital strategy, process engineering, customer delivery, financial analysis, insight generation, and executive\-level program management. **PREPs are placed in high‑impact teams led by OCOO Leadership Team members, including:** * Process \& Operations Transformation (PRIMARY) * Digital Strategy * OCOO Strategy \& Operations * Service Excellence APJ, NA, LATAM, EMEA * Service Excellence Embedded Financing This program aims to attract, develop, and retain early‑career talent, preparing them to become the next generation of OCOO leaders. **Program Structure** You will be part of a global PREP cohort with: * Formal onboarding * Assigned mentors * Soft‑skills and leadership training * Monthly check‑ins and development assessments * End\-of\-project reporting and executive presentations You will participate in rotations across different teams in the OCOO organization, being assigned to specific projects with goal setting and clear metrics of accomplishments through the duration of your stay. Upon graduation after the 1–2\-year program, you would be a candidate to be assigned to a final placement based on your performance, skills developed, profile preference, and business needs. This end‑to‑end structure differentiates PREP as a strategic talent pipeline for OCOO. In this position your focus area will be **Process \& Operations Transformation.** **Required Qualifications** * Recent graduate (Bachelor’s or Master’s) in Business, Finance, Engineering, Data Analytics, Supply Chain, IT, or related fields. * Valid work permission for Ireland * Ability to work with cross\-functional teams across global time zones. * Demonstrated leadership capability through internships, student leadership, or project work. * High learning agility, self\-drive, and adaptability. * Outstanding written \& verbal English communication **Preferred Qualifications And Competencies** * Experience with process analysis, financing operations, digital tools, workflow mapping, or automation. * Internship experience in financial institutions, operations, strategy, digital, or customer‑facing roles (strongly preferred, given PREP’s rotation opportunities). * Strategic thinking \& systems understanding * Business acumen and data\-driven decision making * Customer\-centric mindset * Adaptability \& comfort navigating ambiguity * Professional maturity and strong relationship\-building skills **What You Can Expect From Us** * Initial extensive onboarding to support you with adjusting to each role and team * Ongoing learning and development throughout the program * Competitive salary and great benefits * Great work\-life balance, including hybrid working and Wellness Fridays initiative **Ready to take the next step?** Open up opportunities with HPE. **Additional Skills** Accountability, Accountability, Action Planning, Active Learning, Active Listening, Bias, Business, Calendar Management, Coaching, Computer Literacy, Creativity, Critical Thinking, Design Thinking, Empathy, Follow\-Through, Group Problem Solving, Growth Mindset, Intellectual Curiosity (Inactive), Long Term Planning, Managing Ambiguity, Office Administration, Policy and procedures, Recordkeeping, Risk Assessment, Software Development {\+ 5 more} **What We Can Offer You** **Health \& Wellbeing** We strive to provide our team members and their loved ones with a comprehensive suite of benefits that supports their physical, financial and emotional wellbeing. **Personal \& Professional Development** We also invest in your career because the better you are, the better we all are. We have specific programs catered to helping you reach any career goals you have — whether you want to become a knowledge expert in your field or apply your skills to another division. **Unconditional Inclusion** We are unconditionally inclusive in the way we work and celebrate individual uniqueness. We know varied backgrounds are valued and succeed here. We have the flexibility to manage our work and personal needs. We make bold moves, together, and are a force for good. **Let's Stay Connected** Follow @HPECareers on Instagram to see the latest on people, culture and tech at HPE. \#ireland \#grads **Job** Administration **Job Level** Entry HPE is an Equal Employment Opportunity/ Veterans/Disabled/LGBT employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, gender, or any other protected category, and all decisions we make are made on the basis of qualifications, merit, and business need. Our goal is to be one global team that is representative of our customers, in an inclusive environment where we can continue to innovate and grow together. Please click here: Equal Employment Opportunity. **Hewlett Packard Enterprise is EEO Protected Veteran/ Individual with Disabilities.** HPE will comply with all applicable laws related to employer use of arrest and conviction records, including laws requiring employers to consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories. **No Fees Notice \& Recruitment Fraud Disclaimer** *It has come to HPE’s attention that there has been an increase in recruitment fraud whereby scammer impersonate HPE or HPE\-authorized recruiting agencies and offer fake employment opportunities to candidates. These scammers often seek to obtain personal information or money from candidates.* *Please note that Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE), its direct and indirect subsidiaries and affiliated companies, and its authorized recruitment agencies/vendors **will never charge any candidate a registration fee, hiring fee, or any other fee in connection with its recruitment and hiring process.** The credentials of any hiring agency that claims to be working with HPE for recruitment of talent should be verified by candidates and candidates shall be solely responsible to conduct such verification. Any candidate/individual who relies on the erroneous representations made by fraudulent employment agencies does so at their own risk, and HPE disclaims liability for any damages or claims that may result from any such communication.*

title: Graduate Sales Executive (Cypriot Market)
description: **About Revolut** People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 70\+ million customers get more from their money every day. As we continue our lightning\-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000\+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution. **About The Role** Our Sales team powers customer growth around the world. From prospecting to acquisition and activation, they own their market segments, solve complex problems, and help more customers discover the value of Revolut with a sharp, solution\-oriented approach. We're looking for a Sales Executive to join our Graduate Sales Programme, which offers an unparalleled opportunity for ambitious graduates, regardless of prior experience, to join a high\-performing, international team. You'll be at the forefront of delivering cutting\-edge financial solutions to customers while building key relationships. You’ll kick things off with a focused bootcamp, complete with workshops, lectures, and hands\-on training to sharpen your commercial and communication skills. Once you're up to speed, you'll dive straight into the on\-the\-job experience by working with real clients. Watch this video from our team members about what goes into being an Account Executive at Revolut. Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch. **What You'll Be Doing** * Outreaching prospects across multiple touch points and channels, with an emphasis on cold\-calling * Developing and executing a robust lead\-sourcing strategy by identifying key verticals, diversifying your prospects base, and maintaining consistent activity levels * Building and managing your own pipeline, from prospecting to closing and account management, maximising adoption and upsell/cross\-sell opportunities * Liaising customer relationships with internal stakeholders to ensure a smooth experience, from getting started to daily usage * Being autonomous to effectively balance your time (prospect vs. close vs. upsell), activate internal resources, and engage your team * Using solution\-based selling to understand customer needs and communicate the value proposition * Becoming a product expert on Revolut Business, its market positioning, and the solutions it offers, backed by relevant, compelling case studies **What You'll Need** * Fluency in English and Greek * To have graduated from university in 2024 or 2025 * Excellent communication skills and articulation over the phone * Impeccable literacy skills (email, LinkedIn outreach) * A drive to launch a career in a high\-performing sales culture * An interest in the financial industry **Nice to have** * Previous internship or experience in a high\-performance, fast\-paced environment, with exposure to strategic thinking, analytical tasks, or client\-facing work Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D\&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third\-party services or platforms for our recruitment. * Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain. **We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process.** If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately. By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard\-working team. **Important notice for candidates:** Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles. * Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third\-party services or platforms for our recruitment. * Always double\-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain. **We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process.** If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately. title: Guest Experience Expert

title: HR Analyst (Graduate)
description: **HR Analyst (Graduate)** **Duration: 6 months** **Location: Dublin 4** **Role Overview:** The HR Analyst, reporting to the HR Business Partner, supports the administrative, analytical and operational HR activities within a regulated financial services environment. Working as part of a small collaborative team, this role offers broad exposure to the full employee life cycle with an emphasis on data accuracy, HR systems, reporting, and continuous process improvement. This is a varied role that involves regular interaction with stakeholders at all levels of the organisation, contribution to HR initiatives and projects, and the use of HR data and technology to support evidence‑based decision‑making. The role provides an excellent opportunity to develop capability in HR operations, analytics, and systems within a regulated environment. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys combining HR operations with data, systems, and continuous improvement, and who is motivated by making HR processes more effective through insight and technology. **About the team:** The successful candidate will join a professional HR team of three, consisting of the Head of HR, HR Business Partner, and Senior HR Associate. The team is based on‑site four days a week and works closely with the business to deliver high‑quality HR support to the Dublin workforce, aligned with our organisational values. The team has full ownership of HR activities locally and are supported by the wider HR network within our parent organisation, providing access to additional expertise, guidance, and opportunities for collaboration. As a team, we support one another, are curious, take initiative, act with accountability, and actively look for ways to improve HR processes and our own professional development. **Main Responsibilities:** **HR Administration** * Manage end‑to‑end administration of HR processes across the employee life cycle, ensuring accurate, timely, and well‑structured data is maintained at all stages. * Maintain and continuously improve HR records within HRIS and local filing systems, ensuring data integrity, GDPR compliance, and alignment with internal policies. * Actively monitor data quality within HR systems, identifying discrepancies, trends, or inefficiencies and proposing practical solutions. * Manage centralised HR inboxes, responding to queries efficiently and using recurring themes to identify opportunities for process improvement or automation. **Recruitment Support** * Coordinate recruitment activities end\-to\-end, including posting vacancies, liaising with hiring managers and recruitment agencies, managing candidate applications, scheduling interviews, participating in interview panels and contributing to selection decisions. * Leverage and maintain recruitment data to support efficient, compliant hiring processes. * Maintain recruitment records, ensuring GDPR compliance. * Support the analysis of recruitment metrics and contribute insights to improve hiring outcomes. * Partner with hiring managers and the HR team to enhance recruitment reporting and identify opportunities to streamline processes or improve the candidate journey. **Payroll \& Benefits** * Assist with the preparation of monthly payroll, ensuring accurate and timely delivery. * Act as the point of contact for staff benefits, maintain the local benefits SharePoint site ensuring information is to up to date and accurate. * Liaise with benefit providers to ensure smooth administration, communicate benefits updates and initiatives to staff, track and monitor benefits and leave usage. **Compliance and Regulatory Support** * Support the Fitness \& Probity process \- ensuring regulatory checks, approvals, declarations and ongoing compliance obligations are administered and monitored. * Assist with internal and external HR audits. **Reporting, Analytics \& Process Automation** * Prepare HR reports, dashboards and metrics for management, analysing HR data to provide actionable insights on trends, supporting evidence\-based decision making. * Partner with the Head of HR and HR Business Partner during committee cycles, preparing papers, reports, and responding to information requests as required. * Identify manual, repetitive, or inefficient HR processes and contribute to the design and implementation of automated or technology‑enabled solutions. * Utilise HR analytical systems and tools to extract accurate data to support informed decision\-making across the team and wider business. * Work collaboratively with the HR team and wider HR network to identify opportunities for continuous improvement across HR operations. **Skills and Experience:** We’re looking for someone who brings relevant experience, curiosity, and a collaborative approach to HR work. You don’t need to meet every requirement to apply — if this role interests you, we’d welcome your application. * Clear and respectful communication skills, with the ability to work effectively with colleagues and stakeholders at different levels of the organisation. * Ability to organise and prioritise work in a changing environment, while maintaining attention to detail. * A collaborative approach, with a willingness to take ownership of tasks, share knowledge, and support the team objectives. * A high level of professionalism, discretion, and care when working with confidential or sensitive information. * Experience working in an HR administration, operations or analytics role, ideally within a regulated, financial services, or professional services environment. * Familiar with using HR systems or databases to maintain records and produce accurate information. * Ability to prepare HR reports or metrics and interpret data to identify trends and support informed decision‑making. * Confident working with Microsoft Office suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) for analysis and reporting. * An interest in HR technology, process improvement, and automation, with a practical and solution‑focused mindset. * A relevant qualification in Human Resources, Business, Analytics or a related area, or equivalent practical experience. We are committed to building a diverse, inclusive, and supportive workplace where everyone feels valued and able to do their best work. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities, and experiences. If you require any reasonable adjustments or accommodations at any stage of the recruitment process, please let us know — we’re happy to support you. *All data received from applicants is subject to GDPR and Data Protection requirements.*

title: HR/Payroll Business Analyst

This role will partner closely with business stakeholders, subject matter experts, and technical teams to translate business needs into effective solutions and ensure successful project delivery. **\*12 month contract, Very likely to be extended further** **The Role** As a key liaison between business and technology teams, you will: * Gather, analyse, and document business requirements * Translate requirements into clear functional specifications and process documentation * Identify gaps between business needs and technical solutions * Support stakeholders through testing cycles, training preparation, and go\-live activities * Assist with risk identification, issue tracking, and escalation * Contribute to cutover planning and data\-related go\-live activities * Support change management and post\-implementation stabilization * Collaborate across regional and global teams to align processes * This role offers exposure to multi\-country initiatives and enterprise\-level HR transformation projects. **What We’re Looking For** * Proven experience as a Business Analyst supporting HR, payroll, technology, or process\-driven projects * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills * Experience developing clear requirements and process documentation * Ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines * Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills * Proficiency in Microsoft Office and project/task management tools **Nice to Have** * Experience with HR or payroll systems (e.g., ADP, GlobalView, Safeguard, or similar platforms) * Exposure to global payroll or cross\-country HR processes * Experience supporting change management initiatives * Background in cutover planning or data conversion * Experience in global or multi\-country transformation programs

title: Human Resources Graduate
description: **HR Graduate \- People Department** **Our Story:** The Keogh family have been farming the fertile lands of North County Dublin for over 200 years. Fast forward to today and our family has grown to include more than just flesh and blood; we’ve got an amazing team at the farm, all of whom we consider as family none\-the\-less. We love working with committed and inspiring people to create something our grandparents would be proud of. From our proud family heritage, hard work and passion for quality, we at Keogh’s Farm are committed to producing Ireland’s finest potatoes, crisps, and snacks that are truly… Grown with Love in Ireland. **Purpose of Role:** This is an exciting opportunity for a motivated graduate with internship experience to join our dynamic People team. The role offers hands‑on exposure and real responsibility within a fast‑paced, challenging, and rewarding environment where learning and development are strongly encouraged. As a valued member of the People team, you will play a key role in supporting the delivery of our People initiatives across **HR Processes and Procedures, Values and Culture, Talent Acquisition, and Learning and Development** . This role provides an excellent platform to build practical HR experience, develop your skill set, and grow your career within a supportive and collaborative organisation. **Key Duties and Responsibilities:** * Support the **Talent Acquisition process** , including candidate screening, interview coordination, and managing recruitment correspondence in line with business needs. * Coordinate and support **Employee Induction Programmes** , ensuring new starters have a positive onboarding experience. * Maintain and update **Time Management and HR systems** , ensuring employee records are accurate, compliant, and up to date. * Provide administrative support to the **Performance Management** and **Career Path Development Programmes** , helping to promote continuous development across the business. * Research, organise, track, and report on **training and development activities** , supporting employee growth. * Compile and generate **weekly, monthly, and quarterly HR reports** to support data‑driven decision‑making. * Deliver general **HR administrative support** , including filing, scanning, and managing internal correspondence. * Organise HR‑related meetings, including **agenda preparation, note** ‑ **taking, and distribution of minutes** . * Act as a first point of support for employees by assisting the People team with **HR queries and resolutions** . * Support the **implementation and ongoing maintenance of HR policies and procedures** , ensuring clear communication and consistency. * Coordinate the **People \& Values Calendar** and contribute to various **wellbeing initiatives** across the organisation. * Provide flexible support across HR operations, taking on **additional administrative duties** as business needs evolve. **The Ideal Candidate will possess the following knowledge and experience:** * A **third** ‑ **level qualification in Human Resources** or a related discipline. * **6 months to 1 year of experience** in a similar role or relevant internship is desirable. * Strong proficiency in **Microsoft Office applications** . * Excellent **interpersonal and communication skills** , with the ability to engage effectively at all levels of the organisation. * Well‑developed **organisational skills** , with the ability to manage priorities and meet deadlines. * Strong **analytical abilities** with a high standard of **report writing** . * Clear and effective **verbal and written communication skills** . * A **creative, innovative, and proactive approach** to tasks and initiatives. Please email your CV and cover letter to peopleteam@keoghs.ie ***Keogh’s Crisps is an Equal Opportunities Employer***

title: IT Internal Auditor
description: Because our tomorrow is in your hands. Finance, our way. Finance at Primark Our finance team is renowned for their attention to detail. You’ll enable us to navigate the financial landscape with precision, contributing to the strategic decisions that support our growth. Surround yourself with experts and bring your scientific mind to shape the future of our organisation. **What You’ll Do** In your role, you’ll collaborate with different people across a range of skillsets. Here’s a flavour of your day\-to\-day: * Partner with stakeholders across the business to define the IT risk universe and shape a robust, risk\-based IT audit plan * Lead and continuously evolve the IT audit approach, driving best practice and consistency across the organisation * Deliver a wide range of IT audit and risk engagements, working closely with Internal Audit colleagues and third\-party providers, and embedding data analytics into audit activity * Design and execute IT audit programmes that assess key business, operational and regulatory risks * Provide IT risk advice and subject\-matter expertise to support projects, system implementations and change initiatives * Lead IT investigations and post\-incident reviews, collaborating with the wider business to strengthen controls and improve risk management quality **What You’ll Get** The work you do shapes the impact we have. We’re focused on providing you with the culture to reach your potential. Your wellbeing is our top priority. Let’s talk lifestyle: Healthcare, pension, and potential bonus. 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Bring** We want every person in our organisation to feel like they’re making a key contribution. Here are some of the experiences and skills you’ll need for the role: * At least 3 years’ experience in IT audit, IT assurance, IT risk or IT security, supported by a third\-level qualification in a related discipline (professional certifications such as CISA, CISM, CISSP, ITIL or accountancy qualifications are desirable but not essential) * Excellent attention to detail, time management and organisational skills, with the ability to prioritise, multitask and consistently meet deadlines * Strong analytical capability, including the ability to interrogate large volumes of data while maintaining a clear, high\-level risk perspective * Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, with confidence presenting complex information clearly to diverse business audiences * A collaborative, professional approach with proven ability to build relationships, influence outcomes, coach others and manage sensitive, confidential matters with integrity * Sound judgement, commercial awareness and a value‑add mindset, demonstrating high ethical standards, diplomacy and accountability in everything you do Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business a over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. **REQ ID:** JR\-7947

title: IT Support Administrator (all genders) - Vietnam based
description: **Hrs As a Company** HRS, a pioneer in business travel, aims to elevate every stay through innovative technology. With over 50 years of experience, their digital platform, driven by ProcureTech, TravelTech, and FinTech, transforms how companies and travelers Stay, Work, and Pay. ProcureTech digitally revolutionizes lodging procurement, connecting corporations and suppliers in a cutting\-edge ecosystem. This enables seamless efficiency and automation, surpassing travelers' expectations. TravelTech redefines the online lodging experience, offering personalized content from selection to check\-in, ensuring an unparalleled journey for corporate travelers. In FinTech, HRS introduces advancements like mobile banking and digital payments, turning corporate back offices into touchless lodging enablers, eliminating legacy cost barriers. The innovative 2\-click book\-to\-pay feature streamlines interactions for travelers and hoteliers. Combining these technology propositions, HRS unlocks exponential catalyst effects. Their data\-driven focus delivers value\-added services and high\-return network effects, creating substantial customer value. HRS's exponential growth since 1972 serves over 35% of the global Fortune 500 and leading hotel chains. Join HRS to shape the future of business travel, empowered by a culture of growth and setting new industry standards worldwide. ****BUSINESS UNIT**** ****POSITION**** We are looking for an engaged and skilled IT Support Administrator (m/f/d) to strengthen our Corporate IT department. In this role, you will provide high‑quality technical support to our employees while contributing to the administration and improvement of our IT infrastructure. The ideal candidate combines strong customer service skills with solid technical expertise and a proactive, solution‑oriented mindset ****CHALLENGE**** * Provide Level 2 technical support for client hardware, mobile devices, and peripherals * Troubleshoot hardware, operating system, software, and connectivity issues * Handle incident escalations from the Service Desk and ensure timely resolution * Support users onsite and remotely, maintaining a strong customer service focus * Manage onboarding and offboarding (accounts, devices, permissions) on site in our Vietnam offices * Support administration of Active Directory / Entra ID, including user and group management * Assist with Microsoft 365 services (Teams, Outlook, SharePoint, OneDrive) * Contribute to basic network support, including LAN/WAN/Wi‑Fi troubleshooting and VPN access * Assist with endpoint security, patching, and compliance using Intune * Participate in infrastructure projects such as system upgrades, rollouts, and office IT deployments * Collaborate with senior engineers on improving system reliability and performance * Drive root cause analysis and help reduce recurring issues * Maintain documentation of solutions, processes, and IT assets ****FOR THIS EXCITING MISSION YOU ARE EQUIPPED WITH...**** * 5\+ years of experience in IT support or IT administration * Strong troubleshooting skills for Windows operating systems and PC hardware * Good understanding of networking concepts (TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP) * Experience administering Microsoft 365 and user environments * Familiarity with Active Directory or Entra ID * Ability to work with ticketing systems and follow ITIL\-based processes * Knowledge of endpoint management platforms (Intune, or similar) * PowerShell or scripting experience * Strong communication and customer service mindset * Analytical thinking and structured problem\-solving capability * Ability to handle both operational support and infrastructure tasks * title: Intern, Software Engineer
description: **Job Requisition ID \#** 25WD92001 **Position Overview** At Autodesk, we’re reimagining what’s possible. Alongside technical growth, you’ll develop soft skills and build connections with other interns, locally in your office, regionally across EMEA, and globally, through programs designed just for interns. As a Software Engineer Intern on the Developer Enablement team, you’ll contribute directly to building and scaling applications that utilize environmental data, delivered through a unified standardized interface. You’ll receive mentorship, guidance, and support, while also having the autonomy and responsibility to make a real impact. **Responsibilities** * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to understand the full software development lifecycle * Contribute to real commercial development projects, guided by experienced Autodesk developers * Gain hands\-on experience with TypeScript, AWS, automated testing frameworks, DevOps, and more * Apply Agile and Scrum practices, building your project management skills * Use source control and Continuous Integration platforms such as GitHub and Jenkins * Develop robust APIs to enable sustainability analysis for Autodesk customers * Share your progress and achievements in team meetings, gaining feedback and recognition **Minimum Qualifications** * Currently pursuing a degree in a STEM field (e.g., Computer Science, Engineering) * Expected graduation date: please include your month and year of graduation in your application * Experience with API development (REST and GraphQL), through coursework, personal projects, or previous internships * Programming experience (TypeScript preferred) * Enthusiasm for learning new technologies and a proactive approach to problem\-solving **The Ideal Candidate** * Familiarity with automated testing principles to help write reliable, maintainable code * Familiarity with AWS or other cloud platforms, giving you a head start in cloud\-based development * Additional experience with other programming languages or frameworks is a plus * Strong communication skills and ability to work in a team environment * Previous internship experience in a related field is advantageous Learn More **About Autodesk** Welcome to Autodesk! Amazing things are created every day with our software – from the greenest buildings and cleanest cars to the smartest factories and biggest hit movies. We help innovators turn their ideas into reality, transforming not only how things are made, but what can be made. We take great pride in our culture here at Autodesk – it’s at the core of everything we do. Our culture guides the way we work and treat each other, informs how we connect with customers and partners, and defines how we show up in the world. When you’re an Autodesker, you can do meaningful work that helps build a better world designed and made for all. Ready to shape the world and your future? Join us! **Salary transparency** Salary is one part of Autodesk’s competitive compensation package. Offers are based on the candidate’s experience, educational level, and geographic location. **Diversity \& Belonging** We take pride in cultivating a culture of belonging where everyone can thrive. Learn more here: https://www.autodesk.com/company/diversity\-and\-belonging

title: Investment Data Analyst
description: To provide data domain aligned support to IMG, FFS, and other enterprise data clients and stakeholders to enhance investment decisions and fund oversight. To serve as the point of contact for FFS, IMG and other enterprise data clients for investment data related inquires and partner with internal teams to ensure integrity of investment and fund data. To provide oversight of operational activities to ensure accurate and timely service delivery. **Duties \& Responsibilities** * Provides data domain aligned support to IMG, FFS and other enterprise data clients and stakeholders to enhance investment decisions and fund oversight. Serves as the point of contact for FFS, IMG and other enterprise data consumers for investment data related inquires and partners with internal teams to ensure integrity of investment and fund data. Analyzes and resolves complex data issues supporting Data Management as a process expert per assigned data domain. * Proactively identifies and leads process enhancement initiatives to gain efficiencies and improve quality. Recognizes and resolves roadblocks to maximize team impact. Leverages and strengthens FFS and IMG\-wide network to accelerate issue resolution and to improve quality. Partners with external data vendors and industry contacts to enable best in class data management practices * Develops a strong and consistent knowledge of funds and securities assigned and obtains an end\-to\-end knowledge of the operational processes being supported. Understands details driving the operational processes and the portfolio impacts. Maintains a broad and consistent knowledge of investment management landscape. * Participates in new product implementations and other improvement projects by capturing, documenting, and validating business requirements, ensuring systems are properly set up for go\-live. Communicates appropriately with all necessary clients and stakeholders. * Recommends changes that will enhance workflows and procedures. Integrates new or existing technologies into workflows and communicates updates to all team members, analyzing impact, preparing environment for change, and updating or creating procedures. * Participates in special projects and performs other duties as assigned. **Qualifications** * Undergraduate degree (preferably in accounting, finance, economics, or related field). * Minimum of three years relevant work experience, including one year of project management experience preferred. * Excellent analytical capabilities, research, problem solving, and time management skills required. Proficiency in Data Management procedures and strong knowledge of data domain workflows preferred. * Strong verbal and written communication skills, interpersonal skills, and the ability to build solid business relationships. * Excellent judgment and ability to analyze issues quickly and independently to take appropriate action with minimal supervision. * Strong, demonstrated problem identification, analysis, and resolution skills. * Demonstrated ability to function in a fast paced, ambiguous working environment with multiple and diverse responsibilities. * Advanced knowledge and skills using current versions of the Microsoft Office Suite. Working knowledge of VBA and SQL is a significant plus. * Detail oriented with a high level of energy. Proven self \-starter with ability to work independently and within teams. * Ability to work effectively in a team environment. Demonstrated ability to lead, train, and motivate other crew members **Special Factors** Vanguard is not offering visa sponsorship for this position. **How We Work** Vanguard has implemented a hybrid working model for the majority of our crew members, designed to capture the benefits of enhanced flexibility while enabling in\-person learning, collaboration, and connection. We believe our mission\-driven and highly collaborative culture is a critical enabler to support long\-term client outcomes and enrich the employee experience.

title: Junior Quality Controller
description: Job Req Number: 101473 Time Type: Full Time At DSV we're building the world’s leading logistics and transport network. With this exciting growth comes opportunity — and we’re looking for new talent to join us. **Role:** Junior Quality Controller **Location:** Dublin 11 **Hours:** Monday to Friday, 7:00 to 15:30 We are looking for a Junior Quality Controller to join our Contract Logistics operation. This role supports site\-level quality compliance through audits, document control, and continuous improvement activities. Working closely with the Site and Quality Managers, you will help ensure that operational processes meet internal standards and external certification requirements, contributing to a strong quality and safety culture across the site. **What will you be doing?** * Carry out daily quality inspections and internal audits across site operations. * Support document control activities, including Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and work instructions. * Review and question processes where product or service quality concerns are identified. * Assist with fact\-based problem solving and prioritisation of quality issues. * Support the Quality Manager with site\-wide quality projects and initiatives. * Assist in preparing 8D (Eight Disciplines) and CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) responses for customers. * Ensure documentation is maintained in line with ISO 9001:2015 requirements. * Support continuous improvement by identifying trends and improvement opportunities. * Comply with all company policies, procedures, and governance standards. * Take reasonable care for your own Health \& Safety and that of others, reporting hazards promptly. * Carry out ad\-hoc tasks as required to support site quality objectives. **About You** * Strong understanding of quality and audit processes. * Experience with document preparation and control (SOPs and work instructions). * Advanced Excel skills with strong attention to detail. * Internal Audit certification is desirable. * Analytical mindset with strong problem\-solving skills. * Confident questioning processes and working collaboratively with operational teams. * Organised, methodical, and able to prioritise workload effectively. * Minimum of a high school diploma (or equivalent). * Previous experience working In addition, we can offer access to a selection of employee benefits, such as: * Enhanced Annual Leave Entitlement from your start, which increases with length of service. * Salary Sacrifice Cycle Scheme. * Employee counselling, for support with physical and mental wellbeing. * Company Pension, which, when a member of, also provides access to Life Cover and Critical Illness cover. **Next Steps** If this sounds like the next step for you, please follow the links and apply with your CV without delay! We warmly welcome applications from individuals of all genders, ages, abilities, nationalities, religions, and sexual orientations. Please be aware that all successful candidates will undergo necessary right\-to\-work checks and certain sites require 5\-years’ worth of referencing and background checks. We know that the best way to achieve this is by bringing in new talent, fresh perspectives and ambitious individuals like you. At DSV, performance is in our DNA. We don’t just work – we aim to shape the future of logistics. This ambition fuels a dynamic environment built on collaboration with world\-class team players, accountability and action. We value inclusivity, embrace different cultures and respect the dignity and rights of every individual. If you want to make an impact, be trusted by customers and grow your career in a forward\-thinking company – this is the place to be. **Start here. Go anywhere** Visit dsv.com and follow us on LinkedIn and Facebook.

title: Junior Technician - Transportation Planning
description: **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Dublin.** Join our Transportation Planning Team to work on a large variety of impactful public and private projects undertaken by our key clients. The Transportation Planning Team works on the forefront of projects that directly face the development of sustainable communities and climate challenges. We work on high impact projects that brings global best practice, innovation, creativity and technical brilliance together. Our work builds towards Arup’s purpose which is to ‘shape a better world’ by staying committed to developing solutions that address the climate crisis, nature loss, social equity, the need to transition to more sustainable economies and to bring greater prosperity and opportunity to people’s lives. Transportation is a key economic sector impact in the creation of a resilient society and our team works at the forefront of this field to find opportunities where conventional designs can be influenced. **The Opportunity** To support our continued growth and expansion into new markets we are seeking a junior technician to join our Transportation Planning team to work collaboratively with other engineers, planners and technicians on a wide variety of projects. These projects will typically include new public realm schemes, transport infrastructure and development masterplans. The candidate will work under the leadership of a senior technician who will direct the work to be carried in a fast paced environment where the candidate will be expected to learn as work progresses. The role offers lots of opportunities to advance within the organisation both in Ireland and globally. This is an opportunity to join the Transportation Planning team on the forefront of technical design and to gain on hand experience and technical skills and knowledge alongside experts in this field. There will also be opportunity for structured training and mentorship. **As a Junior Technician At Arup, You Will** * Assist in the design and delivery of a variety of transport projects , collaborating with experienced engineers and multidisciplinary teams. Produce high\-quality detailed General Arrangement drawings, plans and sections using AutoCAD. Production of 3D models using AutoCAD Civil 3D, Revit and other BIM software * Work under instruction given by senior technician or senior engineers working within the transportation team. From time to time you might do work with other disciplines / teams within Arup. * Contribute to discussions on design options available to solve problems encountered * Assist in the development of other team members where required. * Contribute to the development of good practice and processes through involvement with the Arup skills network. * Attend and participate in, presentations and workshops. * In the long\-term, you will strive to understand the design process the engineers utilise within the team, helping the technicians explore the capabilities of various software, and developing ways in which the software can streamline the delivery of our work. **Does this role inspire you?** We're looking for graduates who is passionate about the promotion of and development of sustainable transportation and shaping a better world. **We Also Value a Candidate That Is** * Currently pursuing or recently graduated with an NFQ Level 6 Higher Certificate or Advanced Certificate q ualification in a technical qualification such as engineering, CAD, technical design or similar. * Able to demonstrate a sound technical base * Able to cooperate with others in a team environment effectively and contributes in generating ideas * An excellent communicator who brings teamwork skills and the ability to contribute effectively in a collaborative environment * Working to a plan and quality standards and meeting deadlines set * Understands how to develop self in self, in design, technical and generic skills **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in investing in our people and supporting their growth from day one. As a trust\-owned company, we're driven by our purpose and values, creating a collaborative and rewarding environment for our employees. **Benefits That Work For You** * Professional Growth \& Development: Kickstart your career with our structured graduate development program, benefit from continuous learning opportunities, and receive guidance from experienced mentors. * Global Opportunities: Collaborate with colleagues worldwide and explore potential short\-term or long\-term assignments at other Arup offices and project locations. * Financial Well\-being \& Ownership: Receive a competitive graduate salary and benefits package and share in Arup's success through our global profit\-sharing program. * Wellbeing and Culture: At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme. * Equality, Diversity and Inclusion: We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. \#IREGrad **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?**

title: Labs CSV Engineer
description: **Labs CSV Engineer \- Ireland, Dublin \- 12 Month Contract** Our client is a global leader in pharmaceutical innovation, specialising in the development and manufacture of cutting\-edge drugs. As they continue to grow, they’re seeking an experienced Labs CSV Engineer to join their dynamic team and drive forward groundbreaking projects in Dublin. **What You’ll Do** * Partner with local project teams to deliver Automation and IT aspects of Laboratory capital projects. * Develop and/or review System Development Life Cycle (SDLC) deliverables, compliant with company standards. * Liaise with site AIT group to add input to site SOPs for lab systems. * AuthorReviewApproval of qualification documents for lab systems – RS, CS, RTM etc. * Work with site teams on generation of requirement andor configuration specification and IOQ test protocols. Participate in execution of IOQ protocols for lab analytical instruments where required. ******What you’ll need:****** * BSc in Engineering, Computer Science, and or other technical degree. * Extensive experience in project/automation/IT/CSV/IT and or a similar role with experience in laboratory analytical instrument qualification and system implementation. * Excellent facilitation, problem solving, collaboration and decision\-making skills. * Ability to multitask and to handle tasks with competing priorities effectively and engage with stakeholders. ****If this role is of interest to you, please apply now!****

title: Lawyer - Projects and Construction
description: **Title: Senior Associate** **Department: Projects and Construction** William Fry is seeking a dedicated and ambitious senior lawyer to join our dynamic Projects \& Construction team at our Dublin office. This team provides expert advice on a wide range of construction, energy, infrastructure, and procurement matters. In this role, you will engage in a variety of complex transactions across diverse sectors including commercial development, residential projects, infrastructure, transport, and energy. Additionally, you will collaborate closely with other departments, such as Real Estate, Banking, and Corporate, on construction\-related aspects of transactions, disputes, development projects, financing, refinancing, and M\&A transactions. This is an exciting opportunity to join a high\-performing, and supportive team and work with a broad client base while contributing to the firm's ongoing success. **The Role** In this role you will be responsible for * Providing advice on a variety of construction\-related legal issues, including project procurement, disputes, project management, mid\-stream issues like extensions of time (EOT) and claims, negotiation of construction contracts, and agreements related to project financing and development, as well as construction elements in M\&A transactions. * Drafting and negotiating key contracts and agreements for diverse project types. * Participating in some of Ireland's largest and most complex construction projects, engaging with large Irish and global clients, including developers, contractors, and funders. **The Person** * You have a minimum of 5 years post\-qualification experience (PQE) within a reputable law firm or in\-house legal team, with a focus on construction, energy infrastructure, and/or other major projects. * You are proficient in drafting and negotiating construction contracts (especially RIAI, NEC, and professional appointments) and related documents, with a solid understanding of market requirements. * You possess strong interpersonal skills and have the ability to work effectively within a multidisciplinary team. * You are client focused and have a proven track record of building and maintaining strong client relationships. * You are interested in mentoring and developing junior lawyers on the team. **About us** William Fry LLP is one of Ireland’s leading corporate law firms with offices in Dublin, Cork, London, San Francisco, Silicon Valley and New York. We have over 310 legal and tax professionals and over 460 staff. Our client\-focused service combines technical excellence with commercial awareness and a practical, constructive approach to business issues. We advise leading domestic and international corporations, financial institutions and government organizations. William Fry is an equal opportunity Employer committed to recruiting people from diverse backgrounds and building an inclusive culture which harnesses the unique perspectives and skills of our people to deliver award winning innovative solutions to our clients. We are proud to be accredited as Investors in Diversity by the Irish Centre for Diversity. Please let us know if you require specific arrangements during the recruitment process. We'll do everything we can to support you. All information disclosed will be kept confidential.

title: Lawyer – Corporate (Insolvency Focus)
description: We are working with a top\-tier national law firm based in Dublin for an Associate to join their Corporate Insolvency team. **The Role:** The successful candidate will join a leading Corporate M\&A team, advising domestic and international clients on complex and innovative transactions across the full corporate lifecycle. They will work on public and private M\&A, joint ventures, reorganisations, private equity transactions, and corporate governance matters. They will provide commercially focused legal advice, draft and negotiate key transaction documents, and build strong client relationships while delivering a high standard of service. The role also involves contributing to business development and thought leadership initiatives. **Essential Criteria:** * 2\+ years’ PQE; * Strong corporate law background; * Excellent academics; * Analytical; * Technically excellent; * Strong communication skills; * Flexible and collaborative team player. **Benefits:** * Excellent salary package (commensurate with experience); * Hybrid working; * Generous annual leave; * Professional development; * Friendly culture. Please contact Andrew Currie on 0892015064 or email andrew.currie@achuman.com if you would like to have a confidential discussion about this opportunity.

title: Legal Counsel – Multinational Tech Company
description: My client is looking for an experienced Legal Counsel to join their team in Dublin. They are a major Multinational company growing their team in Ireland at scale. You’ll provide legal advice to management on strategic initiatives, business operations, regulatory matters, and compliance in Ireland. Lead corporate legal activities and ensure timely, effective legal support across the business, including drafting, reviewing, and negotiating contracts. **Key Responsibilities** * Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of commercial contracts and legal documents. * Advise on contractual, statutory, and regulatory obligations, ensuring ongoing compliance in customer engagements. * Conduct legal due diligence and research to support business decisions. * Provide legal counsel across corporate, commercial, and risk management matters. * Identify and mitigate legal and operational risks. * Interpret statutes, regulations, and contracts, and advise on resolving legal and business issues. * Partner with internal stakeholders to deliver clear, practical legal guidance. * Manage and support legal projects and, where required, supervise junior legal staff. * Report regularly to the Head of Legal on key matters and developments. **Skills \& Experience** * Qualified lawyer (LLB/LLM/JD or equivalent) with a current practising certificate. * 5–10 years’ experience, including at least 5 years in outsourcing, IT contracts, or licensing. * Strong drafting, negotiation, and analytical skills, with solid knowledge of relevant laws. * Effective communicator with the ability to work across all levels and cultures. * Strong organisational, project management, and prioritisation skills. * Collaborative, proactive, and able to operate independently with minimal support. * Experience managing or mentoring team members is an advantage. * Willingness to travel domestically and internationally as required. Salary dependent on candidate experience. Benefits: Annual Bonus Scheme. Contributory Pension. Private Medical Insurance. Life Assurance \& Long\-Term Disability. Employee Assistance Programme. 22 days annual leave \+ 10 public holidays. Continuous Learning \& Development. Access to extensive training \& certification resources. Lunch \& Learn sessions. Additional perks including company discounts, on\-site parking, and bike\-to\-work scheme **Based in Dublin, Ireland. Hybrid.** Candidates must be eligible to work in Ireland/EU. For more information, please contact **David Coyle** at **01 635 1748** or email **david@methodius.com**

title: Logistics Specialist
description: **Location:** Ireland (Hybrid) **Salary:** €40,000\- €46,000 (depending on experience) **About Sycomp** Sycomp is a Global Systems Integrator headquartered in Silicon Valley, delivering end‑to‑end IT lifecycle services including logistics, integration, managed services, and global field support for enterprise and Fortune 500 customers. With operations across the Americas, EMEA, and APAC, Sycomp enables customers to deploy and support IT infrastructure at scale with speed, quality, and compliance. Our Logistics team plays a critical role in supporting global device lifecycle programs, ensuring seamless outbound and return logistics, strong carrier management, and on‑time, in‑full delivery across regions. **About the Role** We are seeking a **Logistics Specialist** to join our Ireland‑based Logistics team. This role is responsible for managing outbound and return shipments, carrier coordination, shipment tracking, and logistics administration supporting Managed Service Provider (MSP) and parcel shipments from the Ireland warehouse. The successful candidate will work closely with internal teams, external carriers, and local vendors to ensure accurate documentation, proactive shipment tracking, cost control, and high service levels. **Key Responsibilities** * Full ownership of MSP return logistics, including customer communication, case management, and administration * Issue Air Waybills (AWBs) for outbound MSP shipments, including quality assurance checks of Commercial Invoices (CIs) for exports (e.g., Salesforce) * Maintain accurate case updates with tracking details across all shipments * Perform live tracking of outbound shipments to proactively identify and mitigate delays, manage exceptions, and support OTIF (On‑Time In‑Full) delivery * Act as the primary point of contact for DHL Ireland, managing day‑to‑day carrier interactions * Maintain MSP tracking files, including weekly cost logging and reconciliation of DHL and Omni UK invoices * Arrange and manage E‑waste and disposal requests with approved local vendors * Own and manage locally created Service Requests (SRs) for parcel shipments, with ongoing training on pallet shipments and progression toward full ownership * Perform Oracle Ship Confirm for all outbound orders from the Ireland warehouse * Support additional logistics‑related tasks as assigned by the Logistics Manager **Requirements** * 3\+ years of experience in logistics, supply chain, distribution, or shipping coordination roles * Hands‑on experience issuing AWBs and managing parcel and/or palletised shipments * Familiarity with carrier management and shipment tracking, ideally with DHL or similar global couriers * Working knowledge of export documentation, including Commercial Invoices * Strong administrative and organisational skills with high attention to detail * Experience using logistics, ERP, or order management systems (Oracle experience is an advantage) * Ability to manage multiple shipments concurrently and proactively resolve delivery issues * Comfortable communicating with internal stakeholders, customers, and external vendors * Based in Ireland, or able to work Ireland hours, with hybrid working flexibility *Sycomp is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, color, age, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability status, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic.* *Sycomp will provide reasonable accommodation for qualified individuals with disabilities as needed. If you need assistance or an accommodation in applying, please contact our Human Resources Department at careers@sycomp.com* *Sycomp may use automated tools, including artificial intelligence, to support certain aspects of the recruitment process, such as reviewing applications or identifying relevant experience. These tools assist our teams, and all hiring decisions are made following human review*

That means you already have the core skills of a Recruitment Consultant and now it’s time to use them in a career with real growth, real autonomy, and real earning potential.
